AdditionalDataToAgent.name: AdditionalDataToAgent
AdditionalDataToAgent.summary: This is a set of additional data that may be included when the user sends a message to an agent.

AdditionalDataToAgent.filesToUpload.name: filesToUpload
AdditionalDataToAgent.filesToUpload.summary:
A set of files that user has selected to upload to an agent. This value may be undefined if there are no files
to upload.

AgentAvailability.name: AgentAvailability
AgentAvailability.summary:
Information about the current availability of an agent while a user is waiting to be connected. If these are not set
the Carbon AI Chat will provide generic messaging letting the user know that a request for an agent has been sent.

Note that only one of these fields will be used by Carbon AI Chat if more than one has been assigned a value. Priority
first goes to estimatedWaitTime, then positionInQueue, and then message.

AgentAvailability.estimatedWaitTime.name: estimatedWaitTime
AgentAvailability.estimatedWaitTime.summary: The estimated wait time for the user in minutes. E.g. "Current wait time is 2 minutes."

AgentAvailability.message.name: message
AgentAvailability.message.summary: A custom message to display to the user containing the updated status. This may contain markdown.

AgentAvailability.positionInQueue.name: positionInQueue
AgentAvailability.positionInQueue.summary: The current position of the user in a queue. E.g. "You are number 2 in line."

AudioItem.name: AudioItem
AudioItem.summary: The audio response type definition for future reuse. This is currently the same as MediaItem.

AutoScrollOptions.name: AutoScrollOptions
AutoScrollOptions.summary: Options for controlling how the scrolling occurs.

AutoScrollOptions.scrollToBottom.name: scrollToBottom
AutoScrollOptions.scrollToBottom.summary:
Indicates that the container should scroll to the given "scroll bottom" value meaning the content is scrolled up
from the bottom by that amount. A value of 0 will scroll to the very bottom.

AutoScrollOptions.scrollToTop.name: scrollToTop
AutoScrollOptions.scrollToTop.summary:
Indicates that the container should scroll to the given "scroll bottom" value meaning the content is scrolled down
from the top by that amount. A value of 0 will scroll to the very top.

BaseGenericItem.name: BaseGenericItem
BaseGenericItem.summary:
The base interface that all message response items must implement. Contains common properties
shared by all item types.

BaseGenericItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
BaseGenericItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

BaseGenericItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
BaseGenericItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

BaseGenericItem.response_type.name: response_type
BaseGenericItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

BaseGenericItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
BaseGenericItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

BaseGenericItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
BaseGenericItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

BusEvent.name: BusEvent
BusEvent.summary: The discriminating union of all the possible bus event types.

BusEventChunkUserDefinedResponse.data.name: data
BusEventChunkUserDefinedResponse.data.signature: data: { chunk: PartialOrCompleteItemChunk; messageItem: DeepPartial<GenericItem>; slot?: string }

BusEventCustomFooterSlot.name: BusEventCustomFooterSlot
BusEventCustomFooterSlot.summary: Used to populate custom message footer slots.

BusEventCustomFooterSlot.data.name: data
BusEventCustomFooterSlot.data.signature: data: { additionalData?: unknown; message: MessageResponse; messageItem: GenericItem; slotName: string }

BusEventFeedback.name: BusEventFeedback
BusEventFeedback.summary:
This event is fired when the user interacts with the feedback controls on a message. This includes both the feedback
buttons (thumbs up/down) as well as the details popup where the user can submit additional information.

BusEventFeedback.categories.name: categories
BusEventFeedback.categories.summary: When submitting feedback details, this is the list of categories the user selected (if visible).

BusEventFeedback.interactionType.name: interactionType
BusEventFeedback.interactionType.summary: The type of interaction the user had with the feedback.

BusEventFeedback.isPositive.name: isPositive
BusEventFeedback.isPositive.summary: Indicates if the user is providing positive or negative feedback.

BusEventFeedback.message.name: message
BusEventFeedback.message.summary: The message for which feedback was provided.

BusEventFeedback.messageItem.name: messageItem
BusEventFeedback.messageItem.summary: The message item for which feedback was provided.

BusEventFeedback.text.name: text
BusEventFeedback.text.summary: When submitting feedback details, this is the text the user entered into the text field (if visible).

BusEventHeaderMenuClick.name: BusEventHeaderMenuClick
BusEventHeaderMenuClick.summary:
This event is fired when a user clicks on navigation items in the chat header.
This includes the homescreen button and overflow menu items.

BusEventHeaderMenuClick.clickType.name: clickType
BusEventHeaderMenuClick.clickType.summary: The type of navigation item that was clicked.

BusEventHeaderMenuClick.menuItemIndex.name: menuItemIndex
BusEventHeaderMenuClick.menuItemIndex.summary:
For overflow menu items, this is the index of the item clicked.
For homescreen button, this will be undefined.

BusEventHeaderMenuClick.menuItemText.name: menuItemText
BusEventHeaderMenuClick.menuItemText.summary:
For overflow menu items, this is the text label of the item clicked.
For homescreen button, this will be the back button label.

BusEventHistoryBegin.name: BusEventHistoryBegin
BusEventHistoryBegin.summary:
The event is fired whenever the widget begins processing a list of messages that have been loaded from history.
This event may be fired not only when the history is first loaded but it may be fired later during the life of
the widget if additional messages are loaded from history.

This event is fired when this process begins. This is fired before all the "pre:receive" and "receive" events are
fired which means that the messages here are the original messages before any possible modifications by the event
handlers.

BusEventHistoryBegin.messages.name: messages
BusEventHistoryBegin.messages.summary: The list of all the messages that are being loaded by this history event.

BusEventHistoryBegin.type.name: type
BusEventHistoryBegin.type.summary: The discriminating type of this event.

BusEventHistoryBegin.updateMessageIDs.name: updateMessageIDs
BusEventHistoryBegin.updateMessageIDs.summary:
Indicates that modifications were made to the given messages and that updates to those messages should be saved in
the history store. This is similar to the update behavior of the "pre:receive" event that is handled
automatically.

BusEventHistoryEnd.name: BusEventHistoryEnd
BusEventHistoryEnd.summary:
The event is fired whenever the widget begins processing a list of messages that have been loaded from history.
This event may be fired not only when the history is first loaded but it may be fired later during the life of
the widget if additional messages are loaded from history.

This event is fired when this process ends. This is fired after all the "pre:receive" and "receive" events are
fired which means that the messages here are the potentially modified messages after any possible modifications
by the event handlers.

BusEventHistoryEnd.messages.name: messages
BusEventHistoryEnd.messages.summary: The list of all the messages that were loaded by this history event.

BusEventHistoryEnd.type.name: type
BusEventHistoryEnd.type.summary: The discriminating type of this event.

BusEventHumanAgentAreAnyAgentsOnline.name: BusEventHumanAgentAreAnyAgentsOnline
BusEventHumanAgentAreAnyAgentsOnline.summary:
This event is fired after Carbon AI Chat calls "areAnyAgentsOnline" for a service desk. It will report the value returned
from that call. This is particularly useful if some custom code wants to take action if no agents are online.

BusEventHumanAgentAreAnyAgentsOnline.are.name: areAnyAgentsOnline
BusEventHumanAgentAreAnyAgentsOnline.are.summary:
The result that was returned from "areAnyAgentsOnline". If an error occurred, this will be
HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.OFFLINE.

BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.name: BusEventHumanAgentEndChat
BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.summary:
This event is fired after a chat with an agent has ended. This is fired after BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT but
can be fired both from the user leaving the chat or the agent ending the chat.

BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.endedByHumanAg.name: endedByHumanAgent
BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.endedByHumanAg.summary: Indicates if the chat was ended by the agent.

BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.requestCancell.name: requestCancelled
BusEventHumanAgentEndChat.requestCancell.summary:
Indicates if the chat was ended because the request for an agent was cancelled or an error occurred while
starting the chat. This means the start never fully started.

BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.name: BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat
BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.summary:
This event is fired before a chat with an agent is ended. This occurs after the user has selected "Yes" from the
confirmation modal but it can also be fired if the chat is ended by the agent.

BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.cancelEndCh.name: cancelEndChat
BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.cancelEndCh.summary: This value may be set by a listener to indicate that the process of ending the chat should be cancelled.

BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.endedByHuma.name: endedByHumanAgent
BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.endedByHuma.summary: Indicates if the chat was ended by the agent.

BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.preEndChatP.name: preEndChatPayload
BusEventHumanAgentPreEndChat.preEndChatP.summary:
An arbitrary payload object that a listener may set. This payload will be passed to the service desk
ServiceDesk endChat function.

BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.name: BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat
BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.summary:
This event is fired before the user is connected to a service desk. This occurs as soon as the user clicks the
"Request agent" button and before any attempt is made to communicate with the service desk.

BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.cancelSta.name: cancelStartChat
BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.cancelSta.summary: This flag can be set by a listener to indicate that the connection process should be cancelled.

BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.message.name: message
BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.message.summary: The message that was used to trigger the connection to the agent.

BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.preStartC.name: preStartChatPayload
BusEventHumanAgentPreStartChat.preStartC.summary: Some arbitrary payload of data that will be passed to the service desk when a chat is started.

BusEventMessageItemCustom.name: BusEventMessageItemCustom
BusEventMessageItemCustom.summary:
This describes a custom event that can be authored with the button response type of type "option". When clicked,
this event will fire and provide information authored in the custom event.

BusEventMessageItemCustom.fullMessage.name: fullMessage
BusEventMessageItemCustom.fullMessage.summary: The full message response that contained the button item that triggered this custom event.

BusEventMessageItemCustom.messageItem.name: messageItem
BusEventMessageItemCustom.messageItem.summary: The button item that triggered this custom event.

BusEventPreSend.source.name: source
BusEventPreSend.source.summary: The source of the message being sent.

BusEventSend.source.name: source
BusEventSend.source.summary: The source of the message being sent.

BusEventStateChange.name: BusEventStateChange
BusEventStateChange.summary:
This event is fired whenever the public state returned by ChatInstance.getState() changes.
This includes changes to viewState, showUnreadIndicator, and other persisted state.

BusEventStateChange.newState.name: newState
BusEventStateChange.newState.summary: The new state after the change.

BusEventStateChange.previousState.name: previousState
BusEventStateChange.previousState.summary: The previous state before the change.

BusEventType.CHAT_READY.name: CHAT_READY
BusEventType.CHAT_READY.summary: When the chat has finished hydrating from history or welcome node request.

BusEventType.CHUNK_USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE.name: CHUNK_USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE
BusEventType.CHUNK_USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE.summary: Fired when a new chunk in a user_defined response comes through.

BusEventType.CLOSE_PANEL_BUTTON_TOGGLED.name: CLOSE_PANEL_BUTTON_TOGGLED
BusEventType.CLOSE_PANEL_BUTTON_TOGGLED.summary: When a panel has been closed.

BusEventType.CUSTOM_FOOTER_SLOT.name: CUSTOM_FOOTER_SLOT
BusEventType.CUSTOM_FOOTER_SLOT.summary: Fired when a message with custom_footer_slot.is_on is received.

BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_CLOSE.name: CUSTOM_PANEL_CLOSE
BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_CLOSE.summary: Fired after a custom panel closes.

BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_OPEN.name: CUSTOM_PANEL_OPEN
BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_OPEN.summary: Fired after a custom panel opens.

BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_CLOSE.name: CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_CLOSE
BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_CLOSE.summary: Fired before a custom panel closes.

BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_OPEN.name: CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_OPEN
BusEventType.CUSTOM_PANEL_PRE_OPEN.summary: Fired before a custom panel opens.

BusEventType.DISCLAIMER_ACCEPTED.name: DISCLAIMER_ACCEPTED
BusEventType.DISCLAIMER_ACCEPTED.summary: Fired if the disclaimer is accepted.

BusEventType.FEEDBACK.name: FEEDBACK
BusEventType.FEEDBACK.summary:
This event is fired when the user interacts with the feedback controls on a message. This includes both the feedback
buttons (thumbs up/down) as well as the details popup where the user can submit additional information.

BusEventType.HEADER_MENU_CLICK.name: HEADER_MENU_CLICK
BusEventType.HEADER_MENU_CLICK.summary: Fired when a user clicks on navigation items in the chat header (homescreen button or overflow menu).

BusEventType.HISTORY_BEGIN.name: HISTORY_BEGIN
BusEventType.HISTORY_BEGIN.summary: Fired when history begins to load.

BusEventType.HISTORY_PANEL_NEW_CHAT.name: HISTORY_PANEL_NEW_CHAT
BusEventType.HISTORY_PANEL_NEW_CHAT.summary: Fired when new chat option within the chat header menu is selected.

BusEventType.HISTORY_PANEL_PRE_OPEN.name: HISTORY_PANEL_PRE_OPEN
BusEventType.HISTORY_PANEL_PRE_OPEN.summary: Fired before mobile chat history panel opens.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_ARE_ANY_AGENTS_.name: HUMAN_AGENT_ARE_ANY_AGENTS_ONLINE
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_ARE_ANY_AGENTS_.summary:
This event is fired after Carbon AI Chat calls "areAnyAgentsOnline" for a service desk. It will report the value returned
from that call. This is particularly useful if some custom code wants to take action if no agents are online.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_END_CHAT.name: HUMAN_AGENT_END_CHAT
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_END_CHAT.summary:
This event is fired after a chat with an agent has ended. This is fired after BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT but
can be fired both from the user leaving the chat or the agent ending the chat.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT.name: HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT.summary:
This event is fired before a chat with an agent is ended. This occurs after the user has selected "Yes" from the
confirmation modal but it can also be fired if the chat is ended by the agent. Note that this is not fired if a
request for an agent is cancelled. The human_agent:endChat event however is fired in that case.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_RECEIVE.name: HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_RECEIVE
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_RECEIVE.summary:
This event is fired before Carbon AI Chat processes a message received from a human agent from a service desk.
You can use this to filter messages before they are displayed to the end user.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_SEND.name: HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_SEND
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_SEND.summary:
This event is fired before Carbon AI Chat sends a message to a human agent from a service desk.
You can use this to filter messages before they are sent to the agent.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_START_CHAT.name: HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_START_CHAT
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_START_CHAT.summary:
This event is fired before a chat with a service desk has started. This occurs as soon as the user clicks the
"Request agent" button and before any attempt is made to communicate with the service desk.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_RECEIVE.name: HUMAN_AGENT_RECEIVE
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_RECEIVE.summary:
This event is fired after Carbon AI Chat processes a message received from a human agent from a service desk.
You can use this to update your history store.

BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_SEND.name: HUMAN_AGENT_SEND
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_SEND.summary:
This event is fired after Carbon AI Chat sends a message to a human agent from a service desk.
You can use this to update your history store.

BusEventType.MESSAGE_ITEM_CUSTOM.name: MESSAGE_ITEM_CUSTOM
BusEventType.MESSAGE_ITEM_CUSTOM.summary:
Fired when a button response item with button_type "custom_event" is clicked.
Provides the originating button item and the full message payload to handlers.

BusEventType.PRE_RECEIVE.name: PRE_RECEIVE
BusEventType.PRE_RECEIVE.summary: Fired before a message is received. Can take mutations to the message.

BusEventType.PRE_SEND.name: PRE_SEND
BusEventType.PRE_SEND.summary: Fired before a message is sent to customSendMessage. Can take mutations to the message.

BusEventType.RECEIVE.name: RECEIVE
BusEventType.RECEIVE.summary: Fired after a message is received.

BusEventType.SEND.name: SEND
BusEventType.SEND.summary: Fired after the message is sent to customSendMessage.

BusEventType.STATE_CHANGE.name: STATE_CHANGE
BusEventType.STATE_CHANGE.summary:
This event is fired whenever the public state returned by ChatInstance.getState() changes.
This includes changes to viewState, showUnreadIndicator, and other persisted state.

BusEventType.STOP_STREAMING.name: STOP_STREAMING
BusEventType.STOP_STREAMING.summary: This event is fired when the "stop streaming" button in the input field is clicked.

BusEventType.USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE.name: USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE
BusEventType.USER_DEFINED_RESPONSE.summary: Fired when a userDefined message is received.

BusEventType.VIEW_CHANGE.name: VIEW_CHANGE
BusEventType.VIEW_CHANGE.summary: Fired after the view changes (e.g. when the chat window closes).

BusEventType.VIEW_PRE_CHANGE.name: VIEW_PRE_CHANGE
BusEventType.VIEW_PRE_CHANGE.summary: Fired before the view changes (e.g. when the chat window closes).

BusEventUserDefinedResponse.name: BusEventUserDefinedResponse
BusEventUserDefinedResponse.summary:
Used to populate user_defined responses. Please see the React or web component documentation as usage of this
differs based on implementation.

BusEventUserDefinedResponse.data.name: data
BusEventUserDefinedResponse.data.signature: data: { fullMessage: Message; message: GenericItem; slot?: string; state?: MessageState }

BusEventViewChange.name: BusEventViewChange
BusEventViewChange.summary:
Fires after the view state has been updated in the store. This event is awaited, making it ideal for async operations that should happen after the view change.

**Event Timing:**
1. VIEW_PRE_CHANGE fires (awaited)
2. View state is updated in store
3. VIEW_CHANGE fires (awaited) ← You are here

**Use cases:**
- React to completed view changes
- Run cleanup or follow-up animations
- Cancel and revert the view change (causes immediate revert without firing events)

BusEventViewChange.cancelViewChange.name: cancelViewChange
BusEventViewChange.cancelViewChange.summary:
This is used by the event handler to indicate that the view change should be cancelled and Carbon AI Chat's view should
not be changed. Since the view has already changed when this event is fired, this property will cause the view to
change back. Note that the view change events are *not* fired when the view changes back.

BusEventViewChange.newViewState.name: newViewState
BusEventViewChange.newViewState.summary: The new view state that Carbon AI Chat has switched to. This new state can be changed by the event handler.

BusEventViewChange.oldViewState.name: oldViewState
BusEventViewChange.oldViewState.summary: The previous view state from before the view:pre:change event.

BusEventViewChange.reason.name: reason
BusEventViewChange.reason.summary: The reason the view is changing.

BusEventViewPreChange.name: BusEventViewPreChange
BusEventViewPreChange.summary:
Fires before the view state is updated in the store. This event is awaited, making it ideal for async operations like animations.

**Event Timing:**
1. VIEW_PRE_CHANGE fires (awaited)
2. View state is updated in store
3. VIEW_CHANGE fires (awaited)

**Use cases:**
- Run animations before the view changes
- Modify the new view state before it's applied
- Cancel the view change entirely

BusEventViewPreChange.cancelViewChange.name: cancelViewChange
BusEventViewPreChange.cancelViewChange.summary:
This is used by the event handler to indicate that the view change should be cancelled and Carbon AI Chat's view should
not be changed.

BusEventViewPreChange.newViewState.name: newViewState
BusEventViewPreChange.newViewState.summary: The new view state that Carbon AI Chat is going to switch to. This new state can be changed by the event handler.

BusEventViewPreChange.oldViewState.name: oldViewState
BusEventViewPreChange.oldViewState.summary: The previous view state before this event.

BusEventViewPreChange.reason.name: reason
BusEventViewPreChange.reason.summary: The reason the view is changing.

BusEventWorkspaceClose.data.name: data
BusEventWorkspaceClose.data.signature: data: { additionalData?: unknown; fullMessage: MessageResponse; message: GenericItem; workspaceId?: string }

BusEventWorkspaceOpen.data.name: data
BusEventWorkspaceOpen.data.signature: data: { additionalData?: unknown; fullMessage: MessageResponse; message: GenericItem; workspaceId?: string }

BusEventWorkspacePreClose.data.name: data
BusEventWorkspacePreClose.data.signature: data: { additionalData?: unknown; fullMessage: MessageResponse; message: GenericItem; workspaceId?: string }

BusEventWorkspacePreOpen.data.name: data
BusEventWorkspacePreOpen.data.signature: data: { additionalData?: unknown; fullMessage: MessageResponse; message: GenericItem; workspaceId?: string }

ButtonItem.name: ButtonItem
ButtonItem.summary: This message item represents a button that can perform various actions such as sending messages, opening URLs, or showing panels.

ButtonItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
ButtonItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

ButtonItem.alt_text.name: alt_text
ButtonItem.alt_text.summary:
The alt text for labeling the item. Screen readers will announce this text when the user's virtual cursor
is focused on the item.

ButtonItem.custom_event_name.name: custom_event_name
ButtonItem.custom_event_name.summary: A custom event that can be listened to by Carbon AI Chat when the button item is clicked.

ButtonItem.image_url.name: image_url
ButtonItem.image_url.summary: The URL pointing to an image.

ButtonItem.kind.name: kind
ButtonItem.kind.summary: The style of button to display.

ButtonItem.label.name: label
ButtonItem.label.summary: The display text for the link.

ButtonItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
ButtonItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

ButtonItem.panel.name: panel
ButtonItem.panel.summary: The panel options to display in a panel when the "show_panel" button type is clicked.

ButtonItem.response_type.name: response_type
ButtonItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

ButtonItem.silent.name: silent
ButtonItem.silent.summary:
When true (post_back buttons only), the request generated by clicking this button is sent to
`customSendMessage` but not added to the visible chat transcript. Use this when the button's
`value.input.text` is a machine-readable trigger string that you don't want the user to see.

ButtonItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
ButtonItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

ButtonItem.target.name: target
ButtonItem.target.summary: Where to open the link. The default value is _self.

ButtonItem.url.name: url
ButtonItem.url.summary: The URL for the user to visit when the button is clicked.

ButtonItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
ButtonItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

ButtonItemType.CUSTOM_EVENT.name: CUSTOM_EVENT
ButtonItemType.CUSTOM_EVENT.summary: A button that throws an event for your UI to respond to.

ButtonItemType.POST_BACK.name: POST_BACK
ButtonItemType.POST_BACK.summary: A button that sends its value back to the backend.

ButtonItemType.SHOW_PANEL.name: SHOW_PANEL
ButtonItemType.SHOW_PANEL.summary: A button that shows a panel.

ButtonItemType.URL.name: URL
ButtonItemType.URL.summary: A button that opens a URL.

CancellationReason.name: CancellationReason
CancellationReason.summary: Reasons why a message request was cancelled via the abort signal.

CancellationReason.CONVERSATION_RESTARTE.name: CONVERSATION_RESTARTED
CancellationReason.CONVERSATION_RESTARTE.summary: User restarted or cleared the conversation.

CancellationReason.STOP_STREAMING.name: STOP_STREAMING
CancellationReason.STOP_STREAMING.summary: User clicked the "stop streaming" button during message streaming.

CancellationReason.TIMEOUT.name: TIMEOUT
CancellationReason.TIMEOUT.summary: Message request exceeded the configured timeout duration.

CarbonTheme.summary:
A string identifying what Carbon Theme we should base UI variables off of.
Defaults to "inherit". If you are not hosting the chat on a website that is Carbon styles, you will want to choose
once of the non-inherited values to inject the correct CSS custom property values into the code. See

CarouselItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
CarouselItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

CarouselItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
CarouselItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

CarouselItem.response_type.name: response_type
CarouselItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

CarouselItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
CarouselItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

CarouselItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
CarouselItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

CatastrophicErrorPanelState.name: CatastrophicErrorPanelState
CatastrophicErrorPanelState.summary: The state information for a catastrophic error panel.

CatastrophicErrorPanelState.bodyText.name: bodyText
CatastrophicErrorPanelState.bodyText.summary: The error body text to be displayed in the `CatastrophicErrorPanel`. Will render markdown if provided.

CatastrophicErrorPanelState.hideRetryBut.name: hideRetryButton
CatastrophicErrorPanelState.hideRetryBut.summary:
When true, the panel renders without the built-in retry button. The consumer is then responsible
for closing the panel by calling `instance.updateCatastrophicErrorPanel({ isOpen: false })` once
their own recovery flow completes.

CatastrophicErrorPanelState.isOpen.name: isOpen
CatastrophicErrorPanelState.isOpen.summary: Whether the catastrophic error panel is currently open.

CatastrophicErrorPanelState.title.name: title
CatastrophicErrorPanelState.title.summary: The error title to be displayed in the `CatastrophicErrorPanel`.

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.name: CdsAiChatContainerAttributes
CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.summary:
Attributes interface for the cds-aichat-container web component.
This interface extends PublicConfig with additional component-specific props,
flattening all config properties as top-level properties for better TypeScript IntelliSense.

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.aiEnabled.name: aiEnabled
CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.aiEnabled.summary: Enables Carbon AI theme styling. Defaults to true.

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.assistantAv.name: assistantAvatarUrl
CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.assistantAv.summary: Sets the URL pointing to a custom avatar for the response author. This image should be a square. If not provided, the default Watsonx icon will be used.

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.assistantNa.name: assistantName
CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.assistantNa.summary: Sets the name of the assistant. Defaults to "watsonx". Used in screen reader announcements and error messages.

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.debug.name: debug

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.isReadonly.name: isReadonly

CdsAiChatContainerAttributes.layout.name: layout

CdsAiChatCustomElementAttributes.onViewC.name: onViewChange
CdsAiChatCustomElementAttributes.onViewC.summary:
An optional listener for "view:change" events. Such a listener is required when using a custom element in order
to control the visibility of the Carbon AI Chat main window. If no callback is provided here, a default one will be
used that injects styling into the app that will show and hide the Carbon AI Chat main window and also change the
size of the custom element so it doesn't take up space when the main window is closed.

You can provide a different callback here if you want custom behavior such as an animation when the main window
is opened or closed.

Note that this function can only be provided before Carbon AI Chat is loaded. After Carbon AI Chat is loaded, the event
handler will not be updated.

CdsAiChatCustomElementAttributes.onViewP.name: onViewPreChange
CdsAiChatCustomElementAttributes.onViewP.summary:
Called before a view change (the chat opening or closing) and awaited before the change proceeds. Use it to update
this element's CSS classes and run open/close animations to completion before the chat shell's inner contents are
hidden. A common pattern is to use this when the chat is closing and `onViewChange` when it opens.

Note that this function can only be provided before Carbon AI Chat is loaded. After Carbon AI Chat is loaded, the
callback will not be updated.

your custom element (width and height or using logical inline-size/block-size).

You can make use of onViewPreChange and/or onViewChange to mutate this className value so have open/close animations.

By default, the chat will just set the chat shell to a 0x0 size and mark everything but the launcher (is you are using it)
as display: none; if the chat is set to closed.

Which Carbon theme tokens to inject. If unset (falsy), the chat inherits tokens from the host page.
Set to a specific theme to force token injection.

ChatCustomElementProps.input.name: input
ChatCustomElementProps.input.summary: Configuration for the main input field on the chat.

ChatCustomElementProps.isReadonly.name: isReadonly
ChatCustomElementProps.isReadonly.summary: Sets the chat into a read only mode for displaying old conversations.

ChatCustomElementProps.keyboardShortcuts.name: keyboardShortcuts
ChatCustomElementProps.keyboardShortcuts.summary:
Configuration for keyboard shortcuts in the chat.
Allows customization of keyboard shortcuts for various actions.

ChatCustomElementProps.layout.name: layout
ChatCustomElementProps.layout.summary: The config object for changing Carbon AI Chat's layout.

ChatCustomElementProps.locale.name: locale
ChatCustomElementProps.locale.summary:
The locale to use for the widget. This controls the language pack and regional formatting.
Example values include: 'en', 'en-us', 'fr', 'es'.

ChatCustomElementProps.markdown.name: markdown
ChatCustomElementProps.markdown.summary:
Markdown rendering customization. Extends the framework-neutral
PublicConfigMarkdown with React-layer custom renderers.

ChatCustomElementProps.namespace.name: namespace
ChatCustomElementProps.namespace.summary:
An optional namespace that can be added to the Carbon AI Chat that must be 30 characters or under. This value is
intended to enable multiple instances of the Carbon AI Chat to be used on the same page. The namespace for this web
chat. This value is used to generate a value to append to anything unique (id, session keys, etc) to allow
multiple Carbon AI Chats on the same page.

Note: this value is used in the aria region label for the Carbon AI Chat. This means this value will be read out loud
by users using a screen reader.

ChatCustomElementProps.onAfterRender.name: onAfterRender
ChatCustomElementProps.onAfterRender.summary:
This function is called after the render function of Carbon AI Chat is called. This function can return a Promise
which will cause Carbon AI Chat to wait for it before rendering.

Like ChatContainerProps.onBeforeRender, it receives the ChatInstance; use it when you need the
instance only after the first render has completed.

ChatCustomElementProps.onBeforeRender.name: onBeforeRender
ChatCustomElementProps.onBeforeRender.summary:
This function is called before the render function of Carbon AI Chat is called. This function can return a Promise
which will cause Carbon AI Chat to wait for it before rendering.

Use it to capture the ChatInstance so you can call instance methods later.

ChatCustomElementProps.onError.name: onError
ChatCustomElementProps.onError.summary:
This is a one-off listener for catastrophic errors. This is used instead of a normal event bus handler because this function can be
defined and called before the event bus has been created.

ChatCustomElementProps.onViewChange.name: onViewChange
ChatCustomElementProps.onViewChange.summary:
Called when the chat view change is complete. If no callback is provided here, the default behavior will be to set
the chat shell to 0x0 size and set all inner contents aside from the launcher, if you are using it, to display: none.
The inner contents of the chat shell (aside from the launcher if you are using it) are always set to display: none
regardless of what is configured with this callback to prevent invisible tab stops and screen reader issues.

Use this callback to update your className value when the chat has finished being opened or closed.

You can provide a different callback here if you want custom animation behavior when the chat is opened or closed.
The animation behavior defined here will run in concert with the chat inside your custom container being hidden.

If you want to run animations before the inner contents of the chat shell is shrunk and the inner contents are hidden,
make use of onViewPreChange.

A common pattern is to use this for when the chat is opening and to use onViewPreChange for when the chat closes.

Note that this function can only be provided before Carbon AI Chat is loaded as it is registered before the
chat renders. After Carbon AI Chat is loaded, the callback will not be updated.

ChatCustomElementProps.onViewPreChange.name: onViewPreChange
ChatCustomElementProps.onViewPreChange.summary:
Called before a view change (chat opening/closing). The chat will hide the chat shell inside your custom element
to prevent invisible keyboard stops when the view change is *complete*.

Use this callback to update your className value *before* the view change happens if you want to add any open/close
animations to your custom element before the chat shell inner contents are hidden. It is async and so you can
tie it to native the AnimationEvent and only return when your animations have completed.

A common pattern is to use this for when the chat is closing and to use onViewChange for when the chat opens.

Note that this function can only be provided before Carbon AI Chat is loaded as it is registered before the
chat renders. After Carbon AI Chat is loaded, the callback will not be updated.
ChatCustomElementProps.onViewPreChange.signature: onViewPreChange?: (event: BusEventViewPreChange, instance: ChatInstance) => void | Promise<void>

ChatCustomElementProps.openChatByDefault.name: openChatByDefault
ChatCustomElementProps.openChatByDefault.summary: By default, the chat window will be rendered in a "closed" state.

ChatCustomElementProps.persistFeedback.name: persistFeedback
ChatCustomElementProps.persistFeedback.summary: Allows for feedback to persist in all messages, not just the latest message.

ChatCustomElementProps.renderCustomMessa.name: renderCustomMessageFooter
ChatCustomElementProps.renderCustomMessa.summary: This is the function that this component will call when a custom footer should be rendered.

ChatCustomElementProps.renderUserDefined.name: renderUserDefinedResponse
ChatCustomElementProps.renderUserDefined.summary: This is the function that this component will call when a user defined response should be rendered.

ChatCustomElementProps.renderWriteableEl.name: renderWriteableElements
ChatCustomElementProps.renderWriteableEl.summary: This is the render function this component will call when it needs to render a writeable element.

ChatCustomElementProps.serviceDesk.name: serviceDesk
ChatCustomElementProps.serviceDesk.summary: Any public config to apply to service desks.

ChatCustomElementProps.serviceDeskFactor.name: serviceDeskFactory
ChatCustomElementProps.serviceDeskFactor.summary:
This is a factory for producing custom implementations of service desks. If this value is set, then this will
be used to create an instance of a ServiceDesk when the user attempts to connect to an agent.

If it is changed in the middle of a conversation (you should obviously avoid this) the conversation with the
human agent will be disconnected.

ChatCustomElementProps.shouldSanitizeHTM.name: shouldSanitizeHTML
ChatCustomElementProps.shouldSanitizeHTM.summary: Indicates if Carbon AI Chat should sanitize HTML from the assistant.

ChatCustomElementProps.shouldTakeFocusIf.name: shouldTakeFocusIfOpensAutomatically
ChatCustomElementProps.shouldTakeFocusIf.summary: If the Carbon AI Chat should grab focus if the chat is open on page load.

ChatCustomElementProps.strings.name: strings
ChatCustomElementProps.strings.summary: Optional partial language pack overrides. Values merge with defaults.

ChatCustomElementProps.upload.name: upload
ChatCustomElementProps.upload.summary:
Configuration for file upload behavior in the chat input.
When `is_on` is `true`, the chat renders a file attachment button in the input area.

ChatInstance.name: ChatInstance
ChatInstance.summary:
The interface represents the API contract with the chat widget and contains all the public methods and properties
that can be used with Carbon AI Chat.

ChatInstance.changeView.name: changeView
ChatInstance.changeView.summary:
Fire the view:pre:change and view:change events and change the view of the Carbon AI Chat. If a ViewType is
provided then that view will become visible and the rest will be hidden. If a ViewState is provided that
includes all of the views then all of the views will be changed accordingly. If a partial ViewState is
provided then only the views provided will be changed.

ChatInstance.customPanels.name: customPanels
ChatInstance.customPanels.summary: Manager for accessing and controlling custom panels.

ChatInstance.destroySession.name: destroySession
ChatInstance.destroySession.summary: Remove any record of the current session from the browser's SessionStorage.

ChatInstance.doAutoScroll.name: doAutoScroll
ChatInstance.doAutoScroll.summary:
Recalculates the chat's scroll position and spacer after an external layout change.

Call this after your custom response component finishes rendering, loads media, or
otherwise changes height in a way the chat cannot detect automatically (e.g. after
injecting content via WriteableElements). The chat will re-pin the last
qualifying message to the top of the viewport and adjust the spacer accordingly.

To scroll to the very bottom of the message list instead, pass `{ scrollToBottom: 0 }`.
The spacer reconciliation pass still runs after explicit top/bottom overrides so pin
geometry remains accurate for subsequent updates.

ChatInstance.getState.name: getState
ChatInstance.getState.summary: Returns state information of the Carbon AI Chat that could be useful.

ChatInstance.input.name: input
ChatInstance.input.summary: Actions for mutating the chat input contents.

ChatInstance.messaging.name: messaging
ChatInstance.messaging.summary: Messaging actions for a chat instance.

ChatInstance.off.name: off
ChatInstance.off.summary: Removes an event listener that was previously added via on or once.

ChatInstance.on.name: on
ChatInstance.on.summary: Adds the given event handler as a listener for events of the given type.

ChatInstance.once.name: once
ChatInstance.once.summary:
Adds the given event handler as a listener for events of the given type. After the first event is handled, this
handler will automatically be removed.

ChatInstance.requestFocus.name: requestFocus
ChatInstance.requestFocus.summary:
This function can be called when another component wishes this component to gain focus. It is up to the
component to decide where focus belongs. This may return true or false to indicate if a suitable focus location
was found.

ChatInstance.restartConversation.name: restartConversation
ChatInstance.restartConversation.summary:
Restarts the conversation with the assistant. This does not make any changes to a conversation with a human agent.
This will clear all the current assistant messages from the main assistant view and cancel any outstanding
messages. This will also clear the current assistant session which will force a new session to start on the
next message.

ChatInstance.scrollToMessage.name: scrollToMessage
ChatInstance.scrollToMessage.summary:
Scrolls to the (original) message with the given ID. Since there may be multiple message items in a given
message, this will scroll the first message to the top of the message window.

ChatInstance.send.name: send
ChatInstance.send.summary:
Sends the given message to the assistant on the remote server. This will result in a "pre:send" and "send" event
being fired on the event bus. The returned promise will resolve once a response has received and processed and
both the "pre:receive" and "receive" events have fired. It will reject when too many errors have occurred and
the system gives up retrying.
ChatInstance.send.signature: send: (message: string | MessageRequest<MessageInput>, options?: SendOptions) => Promise<void>

ChatInstance.serviceDesk.name: serviceDesk
ChatInstance.serviceDesk.summary: Actions that are related to a service desk integration.

ChatInstance.updateCatastrophicErrorPane.name: updateCatastrophicErrorPanel
ChatInstance.updateCatastrophicErrorPane.summary:
Fires an event that will open or close the Catastrophic Error Panel in the chat. This also accepts a
custom title and body text (markdown supported) to be displayed in the Catastrophic Error Panel.

ChatInstance.updateIsChatLoadingCounter.name: updateIsChatLoadingCounter
ChatInstance.updateIsChatLoadingCounter.summary:
Either increases or decreases the internal counter that indicates whether the hydration fullscreen loading state is
shown. If the count is greater than zero, then the indicator is shown. Values of "increase" or "decrease" will
increase or decrease the value. "reset" will set the value back to 0.

You can access the current value via ChatInstance.getState.

ChatInstance.writeableElements.name: writeableElements
ChatInstance.writeableElements.summary: Returns the list of writable elements.

ChatInstanceInput.name: ChatInstanceInput
ChatInstanceInput.summary: Methods for controlling the input field.

ChatInstanceInput.updateRawValue.name: updateRawValue
ChatInstanceInput.updateRawValue.summary:
Updates the raw text queued in the input before it is sent to customSendMessage.
Use this when you want to manipulate the canonical value while leaving presentation up to the default renderer or,
in the future, a custom slot implementation.

ChatInstanceInput.updateStructuredData.name: updateStructuredData
ChatInstanceInput.updateStructuredData.summary:
Updates the pending structured data that will be merged into the next outgoing MessageRequest
when the user sends a message via the UI send button or Enter key. The updater function receives the
current pending structured data (or `undefined` if none is set) and should return the new value.
Return `undefined` to clear the pending structured data.

This is the primary mechanism for pushing structured inputs (form fields, file references, etc.)
into the active input so they are included when the user hits Send.

ChatInstanceMessaging.name: ChatInstanceMessaging
ChatInstanceMessaging.summary: Messaging actions for a chat instance.

ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessage.name: addMessage
ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessage.summary:
Instructs the widget to process the given message as an incoming message received from the assistant. This will
fire a "pre:receive" event immediately and a "receive" event after the event has been processed by the widget.

ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessageChunk.name: addMessageChunk
ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessageChunk.summary: Adds a streaming message chunk to the chat widget.

ChatInstanceMessaging.clearConversation.name: clearConversation
ChatInstanceMessaging.clearConversation.summary:
Clears the current conversation. This will trigger a restart of the conversation but will not start a new
conversation (hydration). It will also clear any loading indicators UNLESS you have set
PublicConfigMessaging.messageLoadingIndicatorTimeoutSecs to 0.

ChatInstanceMessaging.insertHistory.name: insertHistory
ChatInstanceMessaging.insertHistory.summary:
Inserts the given messages into the chat window as part of the chat history. This will fire the history:begin
and history:end events.

ChatInstanceMessaging.removeMessages.name: removeMessages
ChatInstanceMessaging.removeMessages.summary: Removes the messages with the given IDs from the chat view.

ChatInstanceMessaging.restartConversatio.name: restartConversation
ChatInstanceMessaging.restartConversatio.summary:
Restarts the conversation with the assistant. This does not make any changes to a conversation with a human agent.
This will clear all the current assistant messages from the main assistant view and cancel any outstanding
messages. It will also clear any loading indicators UNLESS you have set
PublicConfigMessaging.messageLoadingIndicatorTimeoutSecs to 0.

ChatInstanceMessaging.upsertMessage.name: upsertMessage
ChatInstanceMessaging.upsertMessage.summary:
Inserts or updates a single message identified by `messageID`. The `updater` receives
the MessageResponse currently stored under `messageID` (or `undefined` when no
message with that ID exists) and returns the message that should replace it.

Calls targeting the same `messageID` are serialized — each call awaits the previous
call for that ID before running. Calls targeting different `messageID`s run
independently.

The `state` argument describes the MessageState the chat records for this
message after the upsert completes; it is applied uniformly to every item in the
returned message. Carbon AI Chat fires BusEventType.PRE_RECEIVE and
BusEventType.RECEIVE exactly when this call transitions the message into
MessageState.COMPLETE from any other state, including the case where the
message did not previously exist. STREAMING-to-STREAMING and COMPLETE-to-COMPLETE
upserts do not fire these events.

If the returned message has no `id`, Carbon AI Chat assigns `messageID`. The
cancellation contract for outbound messages is unchanged — see
CustomSendMessageOptions.
ChatInstanceMessaging.upsertMessage.signature: upsertMessage: (messageID: string, state: MessageState, updater: UpsertMessageUpdater) => Promise<void>

ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.name: ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions
ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.summary: Start or end conversations with human agent.

ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.endConver.name: endConversation
ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.endConver.summary:
Ends the conversation with a human agent. This does not request confirmation from the user first. If the user
is not connected or connecting to a human agent, this function has no effect. You can determine if the user is
connected or connecting by calling ChatInstance.getState. Note that this function
returns a Promise that only resolves when the conversation has ended. This includes after the
BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT and BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_END_CHAT events have been fired and
resolved.

ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.updateIsS.name: updateIsSuspended
ChatInstanceServiceDeskActions.updateIsS.summary:
Sets the suspended state for an agent conversation. A conversation can be suspended or un-suspended only if the
user is currently connecting or connected to an agent. If a conversation is suspended, then messages from the user
will no longer be routed to the service desk and incoming messages from the service desk will not be displayed. In
addition, the current connection status with an agent will not be shown.

ChatShortcutConfig.is_on.name: is_on
ChatShortcutConfig.is_on.summary:
Whether the keyboard shortcut is enabled.
Default: true

ChatShortcutConfig.modifiers.name: modifiers
ChatShortcutConfig.modifiers.summary: Modifier keys required to be held
ChatShortcutConfig.modifiers.signature: modifiers: { alt?: boolean; ctrl?: boolean; meta?: boolean; shift?: boolean }

Chunk.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
Chunk.streaming_metadata.summary: Additional metadata associated with the stream.

CompleteItemChunk.name: CompleteItemChunk
CompleteItemChunk.summary:
Completes a single streamed item before the full response is ready.

Use this to replace a streamed item with its final, corrected version while other
items are still streaming. The complete item should include all data needed to
render the item (including anything from partial chunks). Include
ItemStreamingMetadata.id to correlate with prior partial chunks and preserve
identity. If you are only streaming a single item, you can skip this and send a
FinalResponseChunk instead.

CompleteItemChunk.complete_item.name: complete_item
CompleteItemChunk.complete_item.summary:
A complete message item. If this item was streamed via partial chunks,
you should include ItemStreamingMetadata.id so the UI can preserve identity.

CompleteItemChunk.partial_response.name: partial_response
CompleteItemChunk.partial_response.summary: Change the agent display name and other items on the full response.

CompleteItemChunk.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
CompleteItemChunk.streaming_metadata.summary: Additional metadata associated with the stream.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.name: ConnectToHumanAgentItem
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.summary:
A "connect to agent" item returned in a message response from an assistant. This is used when the back-end
indicates that a user's conversation should be escalated to a human agent.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_available.name: agent_available
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_available.summary: Contains the message to be rendered if there are agents available.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_message_ty.name: agent_message_type
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_message_ty.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_unavailabl.name: agent_unavailable
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.agent_unavailabl.summary: Contains the message to be rendered if there are no agents available.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.message_item_opt.name: message_item_options
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.message_item_opt.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.message_to_human.name: message_to_human_agent
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.message_to_human.summary: A message to be sent to the human agent who will be taking over the conversation.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.response_type.name: response_type
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.streaming_metada.name: streaming_metadata
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.streaming_metada.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.transfer_info.name: transfer_info
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.transfer_info.summary:
When a conversation is escalated to an agent additional information is needed to fulfill the request. This
additional information typically is added by the channel integration and cannot be deduced from the dialog
itself.

ConnectToHumanAgentItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
ConnectToHumanAgentItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.name: ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo
ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.summary: Additional information as part of a ConnectToHumanAgentItem that may be needed to perform a transfer to an agent.

ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.addi.name: additional_data
ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.addi.summary:
Each service desk may require different information to start the connection. It can be account details or
security information. This is a bucket of all the service desk specific properties.

ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.summ.name: summary_message_to_agent
ConnectToHumanAgentItemTransferInfo.summ.summary: An initial set of message items to send to the agent.

ConnectingErrorInfo.name: ConnectingErrorInfo
ConnectingErrorInfo.summary:
This error is meant to be displayed while the user is attempting to connect to a service desk and before an
agent has joined. If this error is generated by the service desk, it is expected that the service desk will
treat the chat as having ended (or never started).

ConnectingErrorInfo.logInfo.name: logInfo
ConnectingErrorInfo.logInfo.summary: An optional value that will be logged to the console as an error.

ConnectingErrorInfo.messageToUser.name: messageToUser
ConnectingErrorInfo.messageToUser.summary:
An optional message that is displayed to the user in the assistant view. If this value is not provided, a default
message will be shown instead.

Note that support for this field was added in Carbon AI Chat 6.7.0. It will be ignored in earlier versions.

ConnectingErrorInfo.type.name: type
ConnectingErrorInfo.type.summary: The discriminating value for this type.

ConversationalSearchItem.name: ConversationalSearchItem
ConversationalSearchItem.summary: A text response generated by AI with an optional list of citations for where the information came from.

ConversationalSearchItem.agent_message_t.name: agent_message_type
ConversationalSearchItem.agent_message_t.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

ConversationalSearchItem.citations.name: citations
ConversationalSearchItem.citations.summary:
Citations are used to connect specific text within a conversational search response with the relevant documents
returned by the backend.

ConversationalSearchItem.message_item_op.name: message_item_options
ConversationalSearchItem.message_item_op.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

ConversationalSearchItem.response_type.name: response_type
ConversationalSearchItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

ConversationalSearchItem.search_results.name: search_results
ConversationalSearchItem.search_results.summary:
In some implementations, the content searched isn't in an accessible URL. For instance, it could be from Milvus or
ElasticSearch. In that scenario, you may populate "search_results". Combine this with
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.search_result_idx.

ConversationalSearchItem.streaming_metad.name: streaming_metadata
ConversationalSearchItem.streaming_metad.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

ConversationalSearchItem.text.name: text
ConversationalSearchItem.text.summary: The returned conversational text. Any HTML/Markdown will be ignored.

ConversationalSearchItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
ConversationalSearchItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.name: ConversationalSearchItemCitation
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.summary: Citations for text generated by an AI to provide the user with relevant source information and context.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.ranges.name: ranges
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.ranges.summary: Optional array of ranges indicating where in `text` the citation is located.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.search_.name: search_result_idx
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.search_.summary:
In some implementations, the content searched isn't in an accessible URL. For instance, it could be from Milvus or
ElasticSearch. In that scenario, you may populate "search_results". This field will allow you define which index in
the search_results array matches with this citation. The end user can then drill into the larger search result to
view it rather than clicking on a URL to actually see the content.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.text.name: text
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.text.summary: Optional explanation text for the citation.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.title.name: title
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.title.summary: Optional title of the citation URL.

ConversationalSearchItemCitation.url.name: url
ConversationalSearchItemCitation.url.summary: Optional url of the citation. May not be a valid URL.

CornersType.name: CornersType
CornersType.summary: The types of corners the chat can have.

CornersType.ROUND.name: ROUND
CornersType.ROUND.summary: Makes the corners on the chat component rounded.

CornersType.SQUARE.name: SQUARE
CornersType.SQUARE.summary: Makes the corners on the chat component square.

CustomMarkdownRenderers.name: CustomMarkdownRenderers
CustomMarkdownRenderers.summary:
Per-element renderer overrides for the React `ChatContainer`. Each callback
receives the parsed token data and returns a `ReactNode` that renders in
place of the default Carbon rendering. Return `null` to opt out of the
override for that particular descriptor — the default Carbon rendering
runs unchanged.

Callbacks fire once per matching element per render pass, including every
streaming chunk that adds or changes the element's contents. When the
underlying element stays in the document but its data changes (a new table
row, more code lines), the same `slotName` is reused and the callback is
invoked again with the updated payload.

CustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock.name: codeBlock
CustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock.summary:
Override the default rendering for fenced code blocks. Receives parsed
code-block data; return `null` to fall back to the default Carbon code
snippet renderer.

CustomMarkdownRenderers.table.name: table
CustomMarkdownRenderers.table.summary:
Override the default rendering for markdown tables. Receives parsed table
data; return `null` to fall back to the default Carbon table renderer.

CustomMenuOption.disabled.name: disabled
CustomMenuOption.disabled.summary: If true, the menu option will be disabled and cannot be selected.

CustomMenuOption.handler.name: handler
CustomMenuOption.handler.summary:
The callback handler to call when the option is selected.
Provide either this or `href`, but not both.

CustomMenuOption.href.name: href
CustomMenuOption.href.summary:
The URL to navigate to when the option is selected.
Provide either this or `handler`, but not both.

CustomMenuOption.target.name: target
CustomMenuOption.target.summary:
The target attribute for the link when using `href`.
Defaults to "_self" if not specified.
Common values: "_self", "_blank", "_parent", "_top"

CustomMenuOption.testId.name: testId
CustomMenuOption.testId.summary:
Optional data-testid attribute for testing purposes.
This allows tests to reliably find and interact with specific menu options.

CustomMenuOption.text.name: text
CustomMenuOption.text.summary: The text to display for the menu option.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.name: CustomPanelConfigOptions
CustomPanelConfigOptions.summary:
Options that change how the custom panel looks. When a header is shown, it inherits styling and behavior from the
configured HeaderConfig (title, assistant name, AI slug, minimize button style, overflow menu, etc.) unless
explicitly overridden below.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.disableAnimatio.name: disableAnimation
CustomPanelConfigOptions.disableAnimatio.summary: Determines if the panel open/close animation should be turned off.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.disableDefaultC.name: disableDefaultCloseAction
CustomPanelConfigOptions.disableDefaultC.summary:
Disables the default action that is taken when the close button is clicked. Normally clicking the close/minimize
button will run Carbon AI Chat's standard close routine (after verifying no view change is in progress). Set this
to true when you want to keep the experience open or handle closing asynchronously; you'll need to perform the
desired close work inside onClickClose.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.hideBackButton.name: hideBackButton
CustomPanelConfigOptions.hideBackButton.summary:
Indicates if the back button in the custom panel should be hidden. When hidePanelHeader is true, the back
button is hidden automatically. When the back button is visible the panel opens beneath the chat header so users
can always access the assistant-level header controls while the panel is active.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.hideCloseButton.name: hideCloseButton
CustomPanelConfigOptions.hideCloseButton.summary: Indicates if the close/minimize button in the custom panel should be hidden.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.hidePanelHeader.name: hidePanelHeader
CustomPanelConfigOptions.hidePanelHeader.summary:
Indicates if the panel header should be hidden. Hiding the header removes the inherited title, AI slug, minimize
button, and back button chrome entirely. Leave this undefined to animate the panel in with the standard header; set
it to true when you need a chrome-free experience (for example, when the panel content provides its own close
controls or you want the panel to cover the chat header without animating the header into view).

CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickBack.name: onClickBack
CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickBack.summary:
Called after the header's back button is clicked. The panel automatically closes before this callback is invoked,
so you can safely run follow-up logic or analytics once the panel has been dismissed.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickClose.name: onClickClose
CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickClose.summary:
Called when the header's close/minimize button is clicked. By default Carbon AI Chat will run its normal close
behavior (which collapses the experience) before this callback fires; set disableDefaultCloseAction to true
if you plan to intercept the event and manage closing yourself. The callback still fires even when the default
action is disabled.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickRestart.name: onClickRestart
CustomPanelConfigOptions.onClickRestart.summary:
Called when the restart button in the header is clicked. Use this to trigger a conversation reset or your own
telemetry when the restart control is surfaced.

CustomPanelConfigOptions.title.name: title
CustomPanelConfigOptions.title.summary:
The panel title displayed in the custom panel header. Left blank by default which causes the configured chat header
title/name to be shown instead. When a back button is visible the inherited header stays on screen above the panel
so this title acts like a breadcrumb; when the back button is hidden, the header fills the panel chrome and this
title becomes the primary heading within the overlay.

CustomPanelInstance.name: CustomPanelInstance
CustomPanelInstance.summary: The custom panel instance for controlling and manipulating a custom panel in Carbon AI Chat.

CustomPanelOpenOptions.name: CustomPanelOpenOptions
CustomPanelOpenOptions.summary:
Options accepted by CustomPanelInstance.open. Legacy consumers may continue to pass
CustomPanelConfigOptions until the next major release.

CustomPanels.name: CustomPanels
CustomPanels.summary: This manager handles fetching an instance for manipulating the custom panel.

CustomSendMessageOptions.busEventSend.name: busEventSend
CustomSendMessageOptions.busEventSend.summary: BusEventSend provides extra context such as MessageSendSource.

CustomSendMessageOptions.signal.name: signal
CustomSendMessageOptions.signal.summary: A signal to let customSendMessage to cancel a request if it has exceeded Carbon AI Chat's timeout.

CustomSendMessageOptions.silent.name: silent
CustomSendMessageOptions.silent.summary: If the message was sent with "silent" set to true to not be displayed in the conversation history.

DateItem.name: DateItem
DateItem.summary:
This is the response item that represents a request for a date which should prompt the client to use a date picker or
similar control to provide a date. There are currently no additional properties of the response.

DeepPartial.name: DeepPartial
DeepPartial.summary:
Utility type that makes all properties in T optional recursively.

This type is useful for configuration objects where you want to allow
partial updates to nested object structures. It recursively applies
the optional modifier (?) to all properties, including nested objects.
DeepPartial.signature: type DeepPartial = { [P in keyof T]?: T[P] extends object ? DeepPartial<T[P]> : T[P] }

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.name: DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.summary:
Options supported by the default custom panel implementation.

When hideBackButton is set to true, any title value defined here will override the title/name in
the main chat header.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.aiEnable.name: aiEnabled
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.aiEnable.summary: Shows the AI gradient background on your panel. Can be used with in concert with showFrame.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.backButt.name: backButtonPosition
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.backButt.summary:
Controls the position of the back button in the panel header.
Use "start" to position it at the beginning in the navigation slot,
or "end" to position it at the end in the toolbar actions.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.backButt.name: backButtonType
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.backButt.summary:
Controls the icon used for the back button. Use "minimize" to indicate the
panel can be returned to, or "close" to indicate the panel will not return.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.disableA.name: disableAnimation
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.disableA.summary:
Determines if the panel open/close animation should be turned off. By default, the panel will animate up from the
bottom of the chat window.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.fullWidt.name: fullWidth
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.fullWidt.summary:
By default, the panel will render at the width of the messages list. If you want to be able to render to a full screen
width slot, set fullWidth to true.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.hideBack.name: hideBackButton
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.hideBack.summary: Indicates if the back button in the custom panel should be hidden.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.openFrom.name: openFromSide
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.openFrom.summary:
When true, the panel will slide in/out from the side instead of from the bottom.
By default, panels animate from the bottom.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.showChat.name: showChatHeader
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.showChat.summary: Make the main chat header visible while the panel is open.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.showFram.name: showFrame
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.showFram.summary: Show a frame with the chat shell background instead of the gradient background for your panel content.

DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.title.name: title
DefaultCustomPanelConfigOptions.title.summary:
The panel title displayed in the custom panel header. When a back button is visible the inherited header remains
on screen above the panel so this title acts like a breadcrumb; when the back button is hidden, the header fills
the panel chrome and this title becomes the primary heading within the overlay.

DisclaimerPublicConfig.disclaimerHTML.name: disclaimerHTML
DisclaimerPublicConfig.disclaimerHTML.summary: HTML content to show in disclaimer.

DisclaimerPublicConfig.isOn.name: isOn
DisclaimerPublicConfig.isOn.summary: If the disclaimer is turned on.

DisconnectedErrorInfo.name: DisconnectedErrorInfo
DisconnectedErrorInfo.summary:
This is used to indicate the state of errors that can happen any time during a chat where the service desk
implementation has lost a connection to the back-end. If this error occurs while the user is waiting for an
agent to join, it will be treated as a ErrorType.CONNECTING error instead.

DisconnectedErrorInfo.isDisconnected.name: isDisconnected
DisconnectedErrorInfo.isDisconnected.summary:
Indicates if the service desk has become disconnected. A value of true can be passed that will indicate that a
previous disconnection is over and the service desk is now connected again.

DisconnectedErrorInfo.logInfo.name: logInfo
DisconnectedErrorInfo.logInfo.summary: An optional value that will be logged to the console as an error.

DisconnectedErrorInfo.type.name: type
DisconnectedErrorInfo.type.summary: The discriminating value for this type.

EndChatInfo.name: EndChatInfo
EndChatInfo.summary: Additional info that may be provided when a chat is ended.

EndChatInfo.endedByHumanAgent.name: endedByHumanAgent
EndChatInfo.endedByHumanAgent.summary:
Indicates if the chat was ended by the agent (or by the service desk integration). If false, indicates the chat
was ended by the user or by Carbon AI Chat.

EndChatInfo.preEndChatPayload.name: preEndChatPayload
EndChatInfo.preEndChatPayload.summary:
Before a chat is ended, a BusEventType.HUMAN_AGENT_PRE_END_CHAT is fired. The payload value assigned to this
event by a listener is provided here.

ErrorType.name: ErrorType
ErrorType.summary: The possible events that may have some form of error status.

ErrorType.CONNECTING.name: CONNECTING
ErrorType.CONNECTING.summary:
This error is meant to be displayed while the user is attempting to connect to a service desk and before an
agent has joined. If this error is generated by the service desk, it is expected that the service desk will
treat the chat as having ended (or never started).

ErrorType.DISCONNECTED.name: DISCONNECTED
ErrorType.DISCONNECTED.summary:
This is used to indicate the state of errors that can happen any time during a chat where the service desk
implementation has lost a connection to the back-end. If this error occurs while the user is waiting for an
agent to join, it will be treated as a ErrorType.CONNECTING error instead.

ErrorType.USER_MESSAGE.name: USER_MESSAGE
ErrorType.USER_MESSAGE.summary: This error is used to report when there was an error sending a message to the agent.

EventBusHandler.name: EventBusHandler
EventBusHandler.summary:
The type of handler for event bus events. This function may return a Promise in which case, the bus will await
the result and the loop will block until the Promise is resolved.
EventBusHandler.signature: type EventBusHandler = (event: T, instance: ChatInstance) => unknown

EventHandlers.name: EventHandlers
EventHandlers.summary:
This is a subset of the public interface that is managed by the event bus that is used for registering and
unregistering event listeners on the bus.

EventHandlers.off.name: off
EventHandlers.off.summary: Removes an event listener that was previously added via on or once.

EventHandlers.on.name: on
EventHandlers.on.summary: Adds the given event handler as a listener for events of the given type.

EventHandlers.once.name: once
EventHandlers.once.summary:
Adds the given event handler as a listener for events of the given type. After the first event is handled, this
handler will automatically be removed.

EventInput.name: EventInput
EventInput.summary: Base interface for an event message that can be used to send control, updates, or action information to the back-end.

EventInput.message_type.name: message_type
EventInput.message_type.summary: Event messages have this as their input type.

EventInputData.name: EventInputData
EventInputData.summary: Input for an event. The name of the event is mandatory. Additional fields depend on the event.

ExternalFileReference.name: ExternalFileReference
ExternalFileReference.summary:
Represents an external file reference — a file already uploaded elsewhere.
Use this when files are uploaded separately and you just need to reference them.

ExternalFileReference.id.name: id
ExternalFileReference.id.summary: File identifier (could be a database ID, UUID, etc.).

FeedbackInteractionType.name: FeedbackInteractionType
FeedbackInteractionType.summary: The ways the user may interact with the feedback controls.

FeedbackInteractionType.DETAILS_CLOSED.name: DETAILS_CLOSED
FeedbackInteractionType.DETAILS_CLOSED.summary:
Indicates the details popup was closed after the user clicked the "X" button to close it or if the user clicked the
feedback button that opened it.

FeedbackInteractionType.DETAILS_OPENED.name: DETAILS_OPENED
FeedbackInteractionType.DETAILS_OPENED.summary: Indicates the details popup was opened after the user clicked one of the feedback buttons.

FeedbackInteractionType.SUBMITTED.name: SUBMITTED
FeedbackInteractionType.SUBMITTED.summary:
Indicates feedback was submitted. This includes both when the details panel is open and submitted as well as when
the user clicks a feedback button and the details are not shown.

FileFieldValue.name: FileFieldValue
FileFieldValue.summary:
Represents a file value in a structured field. Can be either an inline File
object or a reference to an already-uploaded file.

FileStatusValue.name: FileStatusValue
FileStatusValue.summary: Constants for the Carbon FileStatus type because they weren't kind enough to include their own enum.

FileUpload.name: FileUpload
FileUpload.summary: An interface that represents a file to upload and its current upload status.

FileUpload.errorMessage.name: errorMessage
FileUpload.errorMessage.summary: If the file failed to upload, this is an optional error message to display.

FileUpload.id.name: id
FileUpload.id.summary: A unique ID for the file.

FileUpload.isError.name: isError
FileUpload.isError.summary: Indicates if the file contains an error or failed to upload.

FileUploadCapabilities.name: FileUploadCapabilities
FileUploadCapabilities.summary: Upload options. Currently only applies to conversations with a human agent.

FileUploadCapabilities.allowFileUploads.name: allowFileUploads
FileUploadCapabilities.allowFileUploads.summary: Indicates that file uploads may be performed by the user.

FileUploadCapabilities.allowMultipleFile.name: allowMultipleFileUploads
FileUploadCapabilities.allowMultipleFile.summary: If file uploads are allowed, this indicates if more than one file may be selected at a time. The default is false.

FileUploadCapabilities.allowedFileUpload.name: allowedFileUploadTypes
FileUploadCapabilities.allowedFileUpload.summary:
If file uploads are allowed, this is the set a file types that are allowed. This is filled into the "accept"
field for the file input element.

FinalResponseChunk.name: FinalResponseChunk
FinalResponseChunk.summary:
Finalizes the full response and ends streaming.

This provides the authoritative final state of the full message response. It should
include all items that were streamed (and any corrections). For any item that was
streamed, include ItemStreamingMetadata.id to preserve identity and avoid
remounts. The message ID should match streaming_metadata.response_id.

FinalResponseChunk.final_response.name: final_response
FinalResponseChunk.final_response.summary:
The final message response. If this response contains items that were streamed,
those items should include ItemStreamingMetadata.id to avoid remounts.

GenericItem.name: GenericItem
GenericItem.summary:
The basic class for items returned from an assistant as part of a message response. These are the items contained
in the MessageOutput.generic array.
GenericItem.signature: type GenericItem = TextItem<TUserDefinedType> | OptionItem<TUserDefinedType> | ConnectToHumanAgentItem<TUserDefinedType> | ImageItem<TUserDefinedType> | PauseItem<TUserDefinedType> | UserDefinedItem<TUserDefinedType> | IFrameItem<TUserDefinedType> | VideoItem<TUserDefinedType> | AudioItem<TUserDefinedType> | DateItem<TUserDefinedType> | InlineErrorItem<TUserDefinedType> | CardItem<TUserDefinedType> | CarouselItem<TUserDefinedType> | ButtonItem<TUserDefinedType> | GridItem<TUserDefinedType> | ConversationalSearchItem<TUserDefinedType> | PreviewCardItem<TUserDefinedType> | SystemMessageItem<TUserDefinedType>

GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.name: GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions
GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.summary: Options that control the custom content in a message footer.

GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.addit.name: additional_data
GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.addit.summary:
Optional additional data to pass to the render function. This can contain
any custom metadata that the frontend needs to render the footer appropriately.

GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.is_on.name: is_on
GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.is_on.summary: Indicates whether to show the custom footer slot. This defaults to true.

GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.slot_.name: slot_name
GenericItemCustomFooterSlotOptions.slot_.summary:
Identifier for this footer slot, passed to the `renderCustomMessageFooter` render function.

This value **must be unique to this message** — every message that shows a footer needs its own
`slot_name`. Reusing the same value across messages collapses them onto a single slot, so only the first
message's footer renders. A per-message counter or UUID works well, e.g. `` `copy_footer_${id}` ``.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.name: GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories
GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.summary:
If you want to have different categories for positive and negative feedback, you can provide two different arrays.

You may not provide one of the arrays. e.g. you want negative categories but don't care about positive categories.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.neg.name: negative
GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.neg.summary: List of strings for negative feedback categories.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.pos.name: positive
GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories.pos.summary: List of strings for positive feedback categories.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.name: GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions
