GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.summary: Controls the display of a feedback options (thumbs up/down) for a message item.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.catego.name: categories
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.catego.summary:
An optional set of categories to allow the user to choose from. This can either be an array of strings for
both positive and negative feedback or a GenericItemMessageFeedbackCategories object to make different
configuration for both.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.discla.name: disclaimer
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.discla.summary:
The legal disclaimer text to show at the bottom of the popup. This text may contain rich markdown content. If this
value is not provided, no text will be shown.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.discla.name: disclaimerCheckbox
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.discla.summary:
The label text to display with the legal disclaimer checkbox. If this value is not provided, no disclaimer checkbox
or label text will be displayed.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.id.name: id
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.id.summary: A unique identifier for this feedback. This is required for the feedback to be recorded in message history.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.is_on.name: is_on
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.is_on.summary: Indicates if a request for feedback should be displayed.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.max_le.name: max_length
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.max_le.summary:
The maximum number of characters allowed in the feedback text area.
defaults to 1000.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.placeh.name: placeholder
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.placeh.summary: The placeholder to show in the text area. A default value will be used if no value is provided here.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.prompt.name: prompt
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.prompt.summary: The prompt text to display to the user. A default value will be used if no value is provided here.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_n.name: show_negative_details
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_n.summary:
Indicates if the user should be asked for additional detailed information when providing negative feedback. This
defaults to true.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_p.name: show_positive_details
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_p.summary:
Indicates if the user should be asked for additional detailed information when providing positive feedback. This
defaults to true.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_p.name: show_prompt
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_p.summary: Indicates whether the prompt line should be shown. This defaults to true.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_t.name: show_text_area
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.show_t.summary: Indicates whether the text area should be shown. This defaults to true.

GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.title.name: title
GenericItemMessageFeedbackOptions.title.summary: The title to display in the popup. A default value will be used if no value is provided here.

GenericItemMessageOptions.name: GenericItemMessageOptions
GenericItemMessageOptions.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

GenericItemMessageOptions.custom_footer_.name: custom_footer_slot
GenericItemMessageOptions.custom_footer_.summary: Optional configuration for a custom footer slot. This allows you to add custom content to the footer of a message.

GenericItemMessageOptions.feedback.name: feedback
GenericItemMessageOptions.feedback.summary: Controls the display of feedback options (thumbs up/down) for a message item.

GridItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
GridItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

GridItem.columns.name: columns
GridItem.columns.summary: The list of column specifications. This will determine the maximum number of columns that can be rendered.

GridItem.horizontal_alignment.name: horizontal_alignment
GridItem.horizontal_alignment.summary: Determines the horizontal alignment of all items in the grid.

GridItem.max_width.name: max_width
GridItem.max_width.summary:
Sets an optional max width of the component. Options are small, medium and large.
By default, the component will be 100% width of the container.

GridItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
GridItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

GridItem.response_type.name: response_type
GridItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

GridItem.rows.name: rows
GridItem.rows.summary: A list of rows to render.
GridItem.rows.signature: rows: { cells: { horizontal_alignment?: HorizontalCellAlignment; items: GenericItem<Record<string, unknown>>[]; vertical_alignment?: VerticalCellAlignment }[] }[]

GridItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
GridItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

GridItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
GridItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

GridItem.vertical_alignment.name: vertical_alignment
GridItem.vertical_alignment.summary: Determines the vertical alignment of all items in the grid.

HeaderConfig.name: HeaderConfig
HeaderConfig.summary: Configuration for the main header of the chat.

HeaderConfig.actions.name: actions
HeaderConfig.actions.summary:
Custom actions to display in the header toolbar. These actions can overflow
into a menu when space is limited.

The icon property accepts CarbonIcon objects (from @carbon/web-components) or
React icon components (from @carbon/icons-react).

Built-in buttons (restart, close) will be appended after these custom actions if
configured to be shown. You can, of course, disabled those OOTB icons and replace
them with your own.

HeaderConfig.hasContentMaxWidth.name: hasContentMaxWidth
HeaderConfig.hasContentMaxWidth.summary:
Controls whether the header should be constrained to the messages max width
(--cds-aichat-messages-max-width) or go full width. When true, the header
will be constrained to match the message width. When false (default), the
header will span the full width of the chat container.

HeaderConfig.hideDefaultAiLabelContent.name: hideDefaultAiLabelContent
HeaderConfig.hideDefaultAiLabelContent.summary:
Controls whether the default AI label content should be hidden.
The default content is only meant to serve as a placeholder and should be
replaced with custom content using:
WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_CONTENT and
WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_ACTIONS.
When set to true, all the default ai label content including the deprecated
WriteableElementName.AI_TOOLTIP_AFTER_DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT
writeable element will be removed.

HeaderConfig.hideMinimizeButton.name: hideMinimizeButton
HeaderConfig.hideMinimizeButton.summary: Hide the ability to minimize the Carbon AI Chat.

HeaderConfig.isOn.name: isOn
HeaderConfig.isOn.summary:
If the chat should supply its own header. Can be false if you have a fullscreen chat or one embedded into a page and
you want to only make use of the main application header. Defaults to true.

HeaderConfig.menuOptions.name: menuOptions
HeaderConfig.menuOptions.summary: All the currently configured custom menu options.

HeaderConfig.minimizeButtonIconType.name: minimizeButtonIconType
HeaderConfig.minimizeButtonIconType.summary: Indicates the icon to use for the close button in the header.

HeaderConfig.name.name: name
HeaderConfig.name.summary: The name displayed after the title.

HeaderConfig.showAiLabel.name: showAiLabel
HeaderConfig.showAiLabel.summary:
Controls whether to show the AI label/slug in the header. Defaults to true.

There is currently no version of this that does not include the AI theme
blue gradients.

HeaderConfig.showRestartButton.name: showRestartButton
HeaderConfig.showRestartButton.summary: If true, shows the restart conversation button in the header of home screen and main chat.

HeaderMenuClickType.name: HeaderMenuClickType
HeaderMenuClickType.summary: The type of navigation item clicked in the header.

HeaderMenuClickType.OVERFLOW_MENU_ITEM.name: OVERFLOW_MENU_ITEM
HeaderMenuClickType.OVERFLOW_MENU_ITEM.summary: An item in the overflow menu was clicked.

HeaderMenuClickType.OVERFLOW_MENU_OPENED.name: OVERFLOW_MENU_OPENED
HeaderMenuClickType.OVERFLOW_MENU_OPENED.summary: The overflow menu button was clicked (menu opened).

HistoryConfig.name: HistoryConfig
HistoryConfig.summary: Configuration for the history panel of the chat.

HistoryConfig.isOn.name: isOn
HistoryConfig.isOn.summary: Indicates if the history panel should be shown.

HistoryConfig.showMobileMenu.name: showMobileMenu
HistoryConfig.showMobileMenu.summary:
Controls whether the mobile menu options (New chat, View chats) should be shown
in the header when the history panel is in mobile mode.

When true (default), the mobile menu will appear in the header on small screens,
providing quick access to start a new chat or view chat history.

When false, the mobile menu will be hidden even when in mobile mode.

HistoryConfig.startClosed.name: startClosed
HistoryConfig.startClosed.summary:
Controls whether history starts closed and enables state preservation across mode changes.

When false (default):
- Desktop starts open, mobile starts closed
- Resizing between modes resets to default state

When true:
- Both desktop and mobile start closed
- User's open/closed state is preserved when resizing between modes
- Enables external control via: instance.customPanels.getPanel(PanelType.HISTORY).open()/close()

HistoryItem.name: HistoryItem
HistoryItem.summary: A single interaction in the Session History.

HistoryItem.message.name: message
HistoryItem.message.summary: The message represented by this history item.

HistoryItem.time.name: time
HistoryItem.time.summary: Time this message occurred. ISO Format (e.g. 2020-03-15T08:59:56.952Z).

HomeScreenConfig.name: HomeScreenConfig
HomeScreenConfig.summary: Configuration for the optional home screen that appears before the assistant chat window.

HomeScreenConfig.customContentOnly.name: customContentOnly
HomeScreenConfig.customContentOnly.summary: Do not show the greeting or starters.

HomeScreenConfig.disableReturn.name: disableReturn
HomeScreenConfig.disableReturn.summary:
Defaults to false. If enabled, a user can not navigate back to the home screen after they have sent a message to the
assistant. If false, the home screen is navigatable after an initial message is sent.

HomeScreenConfig.greeting.name: greeting
HomeScreenConfig.greeting.summary: The greeting to show to the user to prompt them to start a conversation.

HomeScreenConfig.isOn.name: isOn
HomeScreenConfig.isOn.summary: If the home page is turned on via config or remote config.

HomeScreenConfig.starters.name: starters
HomeScreenConfig.starters.summary: Optional conversation starter utterances that are displayed as buttons.

HomeScreenStarterButton.name: HomeScreenStarterButton
HomeScreenStarterButton.summary: A conversation starter button on the home screen. Currently, only label is provided by tooling.

HomeScreenStarterButton.isSelected.name: isSelected
HomeScreenStarterButton.isSelected.summary: Indicates if the button was previously clicked and should be displayed as selected.

HomeScreenStarterButton.label.name: label
HomeScreenStarterButton.label.summary:
The display label of the button. This is also the value that is sent as the user's utterance to the assistant
when the button is clicked.

HomeScreenStarterButtons.name: HomeScreenStarterButtons
HomeScreenStarterButtons.summary: Starter buttons that appear on home screen.

HomeScreenState.name: HomeScreenState
HomeScreenState.summary: Current state of home screen (currently, limited to if it is open or closed).

HomeScreenState.isHomeScreenOpen.name: isHomeScreenOpen
HomeScreenState.isHomeScreenOpen.summary: Indicates if the home screen is currently open.

HomeScreenState.showBackToAssistant.name: showBackToAssistant
HomeScreenState.showBackToAssistant.summary:
Indicates if the home screen should display a "return to assistant" button. This button is displayed when the user
has clicked the "back to home" button from the assistant.

HorizontalCellAlignment.name: HorizontalCellAlignment
HorizontalCellAlignment.summary: Horizontal alignment values for items in a grid response.

HumanAgentMessageType.name: HumanAgentMessageType
HumanAgentMessageType.summary: These are the human agent specific message types.

HumanAgentMessageType.INLINE_ERROR.name: INLINE_ERROR
HumanAgentMessageType.INLINE_ERROR.summary: There was an error in a message.

HumanAgentMessageType.TRANSFER_TO_HUMAN_.name: TRANSFER_TO_HUMAN_AGENT
HumanAgentMessageType.TRANSFER_TO_HUMAN_.summary: The conversation was transferred to another agent.

HumanAgentMessageType.USER_ENDED_CHAT.name: USER_ENDED_CHAT
HumanAgentMessageType.USER_ENDED_CHAT.summary: The end user ended the conversation with the agent.

HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.name: HumanAgentsOnlineStatus
HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.summary: Represents the different states for the availability of a human agent from a service desk.

HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.OFFLINE.name: OFFLINE
HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.OFFLINE.summary: Indicates that no agents are online.

HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.UNKNOWN.name: UNKNOWN
HumanAgentsOnlineStatus.UNKNOWN.summary:
Indicates that it is unknown whether any agents are available. This may be because the service desk being used
doesn't support the ability to determine this information.

IFrameItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
IFrameItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

IFrameItem.description.name: description
IFrameItem.description.summary:
The description of the source URL. This property is unfurled from the source URL at runtime. It is used when
IFrameItemDisplayOption is set to 'panel' for the preview card to open the panel.

IFrameItem.display.name: display
IFrameItem.display.summary: How the iframe should be displayed.

IFrameItem.image_url.name: image_url
IFrameItem.image_url.summary:
The preview image of the source URL. This property is unfurled from the source URL at runtime. It is used when
IFrameItemDisplayOption is set to 'panel' for the preview card to open the panel.

IFrameItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
IFrameItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

IFrameItem.response_type.name: response_type
IFrameItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

IFrameItem.source.name: source
IFrameItem.source.summary: The source URL to an embeddable page

IFrameItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
IFrameItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

IFrameItem.title.name: title
IFrameItem.title.summary:
The title of the source URL. This property is unfurled from the source URL at runtime. It is used when
IFrameItemDisplayOption is set to 'panel' for the preview card to open the panel.

IFrameItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
IFrameItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

IFrameItemDisplayOption.name: IFrameItemDisplayOption
IFrameItemDisplayOption.summary: The different ways an iframe item may be displayed.

IFrameItemDisplayOption.INLINE.name: INLINE
IFrameItemDisplayOption.INLINE.summary: The iframe is displayed inline in the main message list.

IFrameItemDisplayOption.PANEL.name: PANEL
IFrameItemDisplayOption.PANEL.summary: The iframe is displayed in a separate panel after showing a card in the main message list.

ImageItem.name: ImageItem
ImageItem.summary: The image response type definition. This is currently the same as MediaItem.

InlineErrorItem.name: InlineErrorItem
InlineErrorItem.summary: An inline error response generated by a conversational skill provider with a user-friendly text and developer debug info.

InlineErrorItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
InlineErrorItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

InlineErrorItem.debug.name: debug
InlineErrorItem.debug.summary:
Relevant debug info intended to be passed on to developers.

This information should not include anything sensitive that might reveal details about our back-end environment that should not be public.
InlineErrorItem.debug.signature: debug?: { info?: Record<string, unknown>; statusCode?: number; text?: string }

InlineErrorItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
InlineErrorItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

InlineErrorItem.response_type.name: response_type
InlineErrorItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

InlineErrorItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
InlineErrorItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

InlineErrorItem.text.name: text
InlineErrorItem.text.summary:
Some end user friendly text describing the error and what they should do next.

If no specific text is passed, the client is responsible for fallback generic error message text.

InlineErrorItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
InlineErrorItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

InlineFile.name: InlineFile
InlineFile.summary:
Represents an inline file — the actual File object to be uploaded.
Use this when the file needs to be uploaded as part of the message send.
The widget passes this through to customSendMessage unchanged; actual upload
handling is the responsibility of the customSendMessage implementation.

InputConfig.name: InputConfig
InputConfig.summary: Configuration for the input field in the main chat and homescreen.

InputConfig.isDisabled.name: isDisabled
InputConfig.isDisabled.summary:
If true, the main input surface starts in a disabled (read-only) state.
Equivalent to PublicConfig.isReadonly, but scoped just to the assistant input.

InputConfig.isVisible.name: isVisible
InputConfig.isVisible.summary:
Controls whether the main input surface is visible when the chat loads.
Defaults to true.

InputConfig.maxInputCharacters.name: maxInputCharacters
InputConfig.maxInputCharacters.summary: The maximum number of characters allowed in the input field. Defaults to 10000.

ItemStreamingMetadata.cancellable.name: cancellable
ItemStreamingMetadata.cancellable.summary:
When included on a partial_item, indicates if the stream can be cancelled.
If so, a "stop streaming" button will display in the UI.

ItemStreamingMetadata.id.name: id
ItemStreamingMetadata.id.summary:
An identifier for this item within the full message response. This ID is used to correlate a partial or
complete item chunk with other chunks that represent the same item. This ID is only unique for a given message
response.

ItemStreamingMetadata.stream_stopped.name: stream_stopped
ItemStreamingMetadata.stream_stopped.summary:
Indicates if the stream has stopped which will trigger the UI to respond with appropriate a11y states
and messaging.

KeyboardShortcuts.name: KeyboardShortcuts
KeyboardShortcuts.summary:
Configuration for all keyboard shortcuts in the chat.
Designed to be extensible for future shortcuts.

KeyboardShortcuts.messageFocusToggle.name: messageFocusToggle
KeyboardShortcuts.messageFocusToggle.summary:
Shortcut to toggle focus between the message list and input field.
Default: F6 (standard Windows accessibility shortcut for cycling between regions)

LanguagePack.name: LanguagePack
LanguagePack.summary:
A language pack represents the set of display strings for a particular language.
It defines all the text strings that can be customized for different languages.

LauncherCallToActionConfig.avatarUrlOver.name: avatarUrlOverride
LauncherCallToActionConfig.avatarUrlOver.summary: An optional override of the icon shown on the launcher.

LauncherCallToActionConfig.isOn.name: isOn
LauncherCallToActionConfig.isOn.summary:
If the launcher will have a call to action expanded state. Defaults to false. This feature will be removed in
the next major release of the AI Chat.

LauncherCallToActionConfig.timeToExpand.name: timeToExpand
LauncherCallToActionConfig.timeToExpand.summary:
The amount of time to wait before extending the launcher. If nothing is set then the default time of
15s will be used.

LauncherCallToActionConfig.title.name: title
LauncherCallToActionConfig.title.summary:
The title that will be used by the expanded state of the launcher. If nothing is set in the config then a default
translated string will be used.

LauncherConfig.desktop.name: desktop
LauncherConfig.desktop.summary: Properties specific to the desktop launcher.

LauncherConfig.isOn.name: isOn
LauncherConfig.isOn.summary: If the launcher is visible. Defaults to true.

LauncherConfig.mobile.name: mobile
LauncherConfig.mobile.summary: Properties specific to the mobile launcher.

LauncherConfig.showUnreadIndicator.name: showUnreadIndicator
LauncherConfig.showUnreadIndicator.summary: Controls whether the unread indicator dot shows even when no human-agent unread count exists.

LayoutConfig.corners.name: corners
LayoutConfig.corners.summary:
Controls the corner style of the chat component.

Can be a simple CornersType value to apply to all corners:
```typescript
corners: CornersType.ROUND
```

Or a PerCornerConfig object to control each corner individually:
```typescript
corners: {
  startStart: CornersType.ROUND,  // top-left in LTR
  startEnd: CornersType.ROUND,    // top-right in LTR
  endStart: CornersType.SQUARE,   // bottom-left in LTR
  endEnd: CornersType.SQUARE      // bottom-right in LTR
}
```

Undefined corners in PerCornerConfig will fall back to CornersType.ROUND.

LayoutConfig.customProperties.name: customProperties
LayoutConfig.customProperties.summary:
CSS variable overrides for the chat UI. This is a convienience method, you may also set these properties via CSS.

Keys correspond to values from `LayoutCustomProperties` (e.g. `LayoutCustomProperties.height`),
which map to the underlying `--cds-aichat-…` custom properties.
Values are raw CSS values such as `"420px"`, `"9999"`, etc.

Example:
{ height: "560px", width: "420px" }

LayoutConfig.hasContentMaxWidth.name: hasContentMaxWidth
LayoutConfig.hasContentMaxWidth.summary:
Indicates if content inside the Carbon AI Chat widget should be constrained to a max-width.

At larger widths the card, carousel, options and conversational search response types
have pending issues.

LayoutConfig.showFrame.name: showFrame
LayoutConfig.showFrame.summary: Indicates if the Carbon AI Chat widget should keep its border and box-shadow.

LayoutCustomProperties.name: LayoutCustomProperties
LayoutCustomProperties.summary:
These variables map to CSS custom properties used in styling the AI chat interface.

Keys map to the underlying `--cds-aichat-*` custom properties.

You can use any standard CSS as the value.

LayoutCustomProperties.bottom_position.name: bottom_position
LayoutCustomProperties.bottom_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the bottom of the viewport for the floating container.

Defaults to `48px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.card_max_width.name: card_max_width
LayoutCustomProperties.card_max_width.summary:
Shared token.

Maximum width for card components.

Defaults to `424px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.height.name: height
LayoutCustomProperties.height.summary:
Float layout only.

Minimum height of the chat container.

Defaults to `calc(100vh - 4rem)`.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.name: launcher_color_background
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.summary:
Shared token.

Launcher button background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.name: launcher_color_background_active
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.summary:
Shared token.

Launcher active state background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.name: launcher_color_background_hover
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_ba.summary:
Shared token.

Launcher hover state background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_fo.name: launcher_color_focus_border
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_color_fo.summary:
Shared token.

Launcher focus border color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_default_.name: launcher_default_size
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_default_.summary:
Float layout only.

Default launcher button size.

Defaults to `56px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.name: launcher_expanded_message_color_background
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.summary:
Shared token.

Expanded launcher message background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.name: launcher_expanded_message_color_background_active
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.summary:
Shared token.

Expanded launcher message active background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.name: launcher_expanded_message_color_background_hover
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.summary:
Shared token.

Expanded launcher message hover background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.name: launcher_expanded_message_color_focus_border
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.summary:
Shared token.

Expanded launcher message focus border color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.name: launcher_expanded_message_color_text
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_expanded.summary:
Shared token.

Expanded launcher message text color.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_extended.name: launcher_extended_width
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_extended.summary:
Float layout only.

Extended launcher width.

Defaults to `280px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_mobile_c.name: launcher_mobile_color_text
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_mobile_c.summary:
Shared token.

Launcher text color on mobile.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_position.name: launcher_position_bottom
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the bottom of the viewport for the launcher.

Defaults to `48px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_position.name: launcher_position_right
LayoutCustomProperties.launcher_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the right of the viewport for the launcher.

Defaults to `32px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.left_position.name: left_position
LayoutCustomProperties.left_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the left of the viewport for the floating container.

Defaults to `auto`.

LayoutCustomProperties.max_height.name: max_height
LayoutCustomProperties.max_height.summary:
Float layout only.

Maximum height of the chat container (float layout).

Defaults to `640px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.max_width.name: max_width
LayoutCustomProperties.max_width.summary:
Float layout only.

Maximum width of the chat container (float layout).

Defaults to the inherited value of `--cds-aichat-max-width` (not explicitly set).

LayoutCustomProperties.messages_max_widt.name: messages_max_width
LayoutCustomProperties.messages_max_widt.summary:
Shared token.

Maximum width for message content area.

Defaults to `672px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.messages_min_widt.name: messages_min_width
LayoutCustomProperties.messages_min_widt.summary:
Shared token.

Minimum width for message content area.

Defaults to `320px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.min_height.name: min_height
LayoutCustomProperties.min_height.summary:
Float layout only.

Minimum height of the chat container.

Defaults to `max(150px, calc(min(256px, 100vh) - var(--cds-aichat-bottom-position)))`.

LayoutCustomProperties.right_position.name: right_position
LayoutCustomProperties.right_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the right of the viewport for the floating container.

Defaults to `32px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.top_position.name: top_position
LayoutCustomProperties.top_position.summary:
Float layout only.

Distance from the top of the viewport for the floating container.

Defaults to `auto`.

LayoutCustomProperties.unread_indicator_.name: unread_indicator_color_background
LayoutCustomProperties.unread_indicator_.summary:
Shared token.

Unread indicator background color.

LayoutCustomProperties.unread_indicator_.name: unread_indicator_color_text
LayoutCustomProperties.unread_indicator_.summary:
Shared token.

Unread indicator text color.

LayoutCustomProperties.width.name: width
LayoutCustomProperties.width.summary:
Float layout only.

Width of the chat panel (float layout).

Defaults to `min(380px, var(--cds-aichat-max-width))`.

LayoutCustomProperties.workspace_min_wid.name: workspace_min_width
LayoutCustomProperties.workspace_min_wid.summary:
Shared token.

Minimum width for workspace panel.

Defaults to `480px`.

LayoutCustomProperties.z_index.name: z_index
LayoutCustomProperties.z_index.summary:
Float layout only.

z-index of the chat overlay or container (float layout).

Defaults to `99999`.

MarkdownCustomRenderers.name: MarkdownCustomRenderers
MarkdownCustomRenderers.summary:
Framework-neutral per-element renderer overrides accepted by the
underlying `cds-aichat-markdown` element. The React variant
CustomMarkdownRenderers and the web-component variant
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers extend this contract with their layer's
return type. Application code typically uses one of those variants rather
than this baseline directly.

MarkdownItPlugin.name: MarkdownItPlugin
MarkdownItPlugin.summary:
Element type of PublicConfigMarkdown.markdownItPlugins. Either a
bare plugin function or a `[plugin, options]` / `[plugin, ...params]` tuple
matching `MarkdownIt.use(...)`.

MarkdownRendererCodeBlockArgs.name: MarkdownRendererCodeBlockArgs
MarkdownRendererCodeBlockArgs.summary:
Argument passed to the fenced code-block renderer callbacks on
CustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock and
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock. Extends
MarkdownRendererCodeBlockData with the source token, full
TokenTree node, and a stable `slotName` suitable for use as a key.

MarkdownRendererCodeBlockData.name: MarkdownRendererCodeBlockData
MarkdownRendererCodeBlockData.summary:
Parsed code-block payload extended by MarkdownRendererCodeBlockArgs —
the argument shape the code-block renderer callback actually receives.
Carries the language, code text, and streaming flag.

MarkdownRendererTableArgs.name: MarkdownRendererTableArgs
MarkdownRendererTableArgs.summary:
Argument passed to the markdown table renderer callbacks on
CustomMarkdownRenderers.table and
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.table. Extends
MarkdownRendererTableData with the source token, full
TokenTree node, and a stable `slotName` suitable for use as a key.

MarkdownRendererTableData.name: MarkdownRendererTableData
MarkdownRendererTableData.summary:
Parsed table payload extended by MarkdownRendererTableArgs — the
argument shape the table renderer callback actually receives. Carries the
headers, rows, and streaming/loading flags.

MediaFileAccessibility.name: MediaFileAccessibility
MediaFileAccessibility.summary:
Accessibility features for raw media files (not embedded platforms).
These features only apply when using direct file URLs (e.g., .mp4, .mp3).

For embedded platforms (YouTube, Vimeo, SoundCloud, etc.),
rely on the platform's built-in accessibility features instead.

MediaFileAccessibility.subtitle_tracks.name: subtitle_tracks
MediaFileAccessibility.subtitle_tracks.summary:
Subtitle/caption tracks for video files.
Supports WebVTT format rendered as native HTML5 track elements.

MediaFileAccessibility.transcript.name: transcript
MediaFileAccessibility.transcript.summary:
Text transcript for audio files.
Displayed as expandable text below the audio player.

MediaItem.name: MediaItem
MediaItem.summary:
A reusable media object that may need to display a title and description with an alt_text to label the item for
accessibility purposes. This is used by the Audio, Video and Image response types.

MediaItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
MediaItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

MediaItem.alt_text.name: alt_text
MediaItem.alt_text.summary:
The alt text for labeling the item. Screen readers will announce this text when the user's virtual cursor
is focused on the item.

MediaItem.dimensions.name: dimensions
MediaItem.dimensions.summary: Settings that control the dimensions for the media item.

MediaItem.file_accessibility.name: file_accessibility
MediaItem.file_accessibility.summary:
Accessibility features for raw media files.
Only applies to direct file URLs (e.g., .mp4, .mp3), not embedded platforms.

MediaItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
MediaItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

MediaItem.response_type.name: response_type
MediaItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

MediaItem.source.name: source
MediaItem.source.summary:
The url pointing to a media source, whether audio, video, or image.

For video this can be a file like an .mp4 or a YouTube, Facebook, Vimeo, Twitch, Streamable, Wistia, or Vidyard url.

For audio this can be a file like an .mp3 or a SoundCloud url.

MediaItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
MediaItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

MediaItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
MediaItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

MediaItemDimensions.name: MediaItemDimensions
MediaItemDimensions.summary: Dimension information for displaying a media item.

MediaItemDimensions.base_height.name: base_height
MediaItemDimensions.base_height.summary:
This property's value is used to calculate a responsive height for Carbon AI Chat's media player so that its aspect
ratio is the same between different screen widths. This is set to a reasonable default depending on the response type
and other details like what service you are pulling the content from (e.g. Youtube or SoundCloud).

MediaSubtitleTrack.name: MediaSubtitleTrack
MediaSubtitleTrack.summary:
Represents a single subtitle/caption track for video content.
Uses WebVTT format for accessibility. Rendered as native HTML5 track elements.

MediaSubtitleTrack.default.name: default
MediaSubtitleTrack.default.summary:
Whether this track should be enabled by default.
Only one track should be default.

MediaSubtitleTrack.kind.name: kind
MediaSubtitleTrack.kind.summary:
The kind of text track.
- "subtitles": Translation of dialogue (default)
- "captions": Transcription including sound effects
- "descriptions": Audio descriptions for visually impaired

MediaSubtitleTrack.label.name: label
MediaSubtitleTrack.label.summary:
Human-readable label for the track (e.g., "English", "Spanish").
Displayed in the browser's subtitle menu.

MediaSubtitleTrack.language.name: language
MediaSubtitleTrack.language.summary:
The language code (e.g., "en", "es", "fr").
Used for the track's srclang attribute.

MediaSubtitleTrack.src.name: src
MediaSubtitleTrack.src.summary: URL pointing to the WebVTT subtitle file.

MediaTranscript.name: MediaTranscript
MediaTranscript.summary:
Represents a text transcript for audio content.
Displayed as readable text below the audio player for accessibility.

MediaTranscript.label.name: label
MediaTranscript.label.summary: Optional label for the transcript (e.g., "English Transcript").

MediaTranscript.text.name: text
MediaTranscript.text.summary:
Full text transcript of the audio content.
Supports markdown for formatting.

Message.name: Message
Message.summary: A general type to indicate any message.

MessageErrorState.name: MessageErrorState
MessageErrorState.summary: The different type of error states a given message can be in.

MessageErrorState.FAILED.name: FAILED
MessageErrorState.FAILED.summary: The message failed to be sent and no more attempts will be made.

MessageErrorState.RETRYING.name: RETRYING
MessageErrorState.RETRYING.summary: There was an error sending the message but the system is retrying the message.

MessageErrorState.WAITING.name: WAITING
MessageErrorState.WAITING.summary:
Indicates that the previous message has entered the retrying state and that this message is waiting for it to
finish or fail. This message will remain in the waiting state until it finishes successfully or it enters a
retrying state itself.

MessageHistoryFeedback.categories.name: categories
MessageHistoryFeedback.categories.summary: When submitting feedback details, this is the list of categories the user selected (if visible).

MessageHistoryFeedback.is_positive.name: is_positive
MessageHistoryFeedback.is_positive.summary: Indicates if positive feedback was provided.

MessageHistoryFeedback.text.name: text
MessageHistoryFeedback.text.summary: The feedback text provided by the user.

MessageInput.name: MessageInput
MessageInput.summary:
The default interface for message input that is sent to an assistant in a message request. This represents basic text
input.

MessageInput.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
MessageInput.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

MessageInput.structured_data.name: structured_data
MessageInput.structured_data.summary:
Structured data that can be sent alongside or instead of plain text input.
Supports typed fields (text, select, multi-select, file, etc.) and an
escape hatch for arbitrary user-defined data.

MessageInput.text.name: text
MessageInput.text.summary: The text of the user input to send to the back-end.

MessageInputType.name: MessageInputType
MessageInputType.summary: The set of possible message input types in a request.

MessageInputType.EVENT.name: EVENT
MessageInputType.EVENT.summary: Represents an event message that can be used to send control, updates, or action information to the back-end.

MessageItemPanelInfo.ai_enabled.name: ai_enabled
MessageItemPanelInfo.ai_enabled.summary:
Shows the AI gradient background on your panel. Can be used with in concert with showFrame.

Defaults to PublicConfig.aiEnabled value.

MessageItemPanelInfo.body.name: body
MessageItemPanelInfo.body.summary: A list of message items to render in a Carbon AI Chat panel.

MessageItemPanelInfo.footer.name: footer
MessageItemPanelInfo.footer.summary: A list of button items that are rendered under the panel body.

MessageItemPanelInfo.full_width.name: full_width
MessageItemPanelInfo.full_width.summary:
By default, the panel will render at the width of the messages list. If you want to be able to render to a full screen
width slot, set full_width to true.

Defaults to false.

MessageItemPanelInfo.show_animations.name: show_animations
MessageItemPanelInfo.show_animations.summary:
Determines if the panel close and open animations should be enabled or not.

Defaults to true.

MessageItemPanelInfo.show_frame.name: show_frame
MessageItemPanelInfo.show_frame.summary:
Show a frame with the chat shell background instead of the gradient background for your panel content.

Defaults to true.

MessageItemPanelInfo.show_header.name: show_header
MessageItemPanelInfo.show_header.summary:
Determines if the panel header should not be visible or not.

Defaults to true.

MessageItemPanelInfo.title.name: title
MessageItemPanelInfo.title.summary: The title to give the panel in Carbon AI Chat.

MessageOutput.name: MessageOutput
MessageOutput.summary: The output context for a message response from an assistant.

MessageOutput.generic.name: generic
MessageOutput.generic.summary: Responses intended to be processed by a generic channel. This will be an array of message response items.

MessageRequest.name: MessageRequest
MessageRequest.summary: This is the main interface that represents a request from a user sent to an assistant.

MessageRequest.context.name: context
MessageRequest.context.summary: Optional context which is added from external resources.

MessageRequest.history.name: history
MessageRequest.history.summary:
The history information to store as part of this request. This includes extra information that was provided to
the user and about the user that was used in making the request.

MessageRequest.id.name: id
MessageRequest.id.summary:
The unique identifier for this request object. This value may be assigned by the client when a request is
made but should be assigned by the service if one is not provided.

MessageRequest.input.name: input
MessageRequest.input.summary: The input data to the back-end to make in this request.

MessageRequest.parent_thread_id.name: parent_thread_id
MessageRequest.parent_thread_id.summary:
The parent ID of the thread this request belongs to. This is here to prepare for input message editing and regenerating
responses.

MessageRequest.thread_id.name: thread_id
MessageRequest.thread_id.summary:
The ID of the thread this request belongs to. This is here to prepare for input message editing and regenerating
responses.

MessageRequestHistory.name: MessageRequestHistory
MessageRequestHistory.summary:
This interface contains information about the history of a given MessageRequest. This information should be
saved your history store.

MessageRequestHistory.error_state.name: error_state
MessageRequestHistory.error_state.summary: The error state of this message.

MessageRequestHistory.is_welcome_request.name: is_welcome_request
MessageRequestHistory.is_welcome_request.summary: If the message was a welcome node request.

MessageRequestHistory.label.name: label
MessageRequestHistory.label.summary:
The user-friendly label that was associated with this message. This is used on messages that were sent by the
user to the assistant to request a response. This is the user displayed text that was entered or selected by
the user when that request was made. Most commonly used to make sure a OptionItem shows the correct button
selected when loading history.

MessageRequestHistory.related_message_id.name: related_message_id
MessageRequestHistory.related_message_id.summary:
If this message is related to another message, this is the ID of that other message. This is used when a user
choices an option and it includes the ID of the message response that presented the options to the user so we
can associate the user's request with that earlier response and display the appropriate selected state.

MessageRequestHistory.silent.name: silent
MessageRequestHistory.silent.summary:
Indicates if this is a "silent" message. These messages are sent to or received from the assistant but should
not be displayed to the user.

MessageRequestHistory.timestamp.name: timestamp
MessageRequestHistory.timestamp.summary: The time at which this message occurred.

MessageResponse.name: MessageResponse
MessageResponse.summary:
This interface represents the main response content that is received by a client from an assistant. It is generally
in response to a previous message request.

MessageResponse.context.name: context
MessageResponse.context.summary: The context information returned by the back-end.

MessageResponse.history.name: history
MessageResponse.history.summary: The history information to store as part of this request.

MessageResponse.id.name: id
MessageResponse.id.summary: A unique identifier for this response object.

MessageResponse.message_options.name: message_options
MessageResponse.message_options.summary: Options for the MessageResponse. This includes metadata about the user or assistant sending this response.

MessageResponse.output.name: output
MessageResponse.output.summary: The output from the back-end to be rendered or processed by the client.

MessageResponse.request_id.name: request_id
MessageResponse.request_id.summary: The id of the request that this is the response of.

MessageResponse.thread_id.name: thread_id
MessageResponse.thread_id.summary:
The ID of the thread this request belongs to. This is here to prepare for input message editing and regenerating
responses.

MessageResponseHistory.name: MessageResponseHistory
MessageResponseHistory.summary:
This interface contains information about the history of a given MessageResponse. This information should be
saved your history store.

MessageResponseHistory.error_state.name: error_state
MessageResponseHistory.error_state.summary: The error state of this message.

MessageResponseHistory.feedback.name: feedback
MessageResponseHistory.feedback.summary: The state of feedback provided on the items in this message.

MessageResponseHistory.silent.name: silent
MessageResponseHistory.silent.summary:
Indicates if this is a "silent" message. These messages are sent to or received from the assistant but should
not be displayed to the user.

MessageResponseHistory.timestamp.name: timestamp
MessageResponseHistory.timestamp.summary: The time at which this message occurred.

MessageResponseOptions.name: MessageResponseOptions
MessageResponseOptions.summary: This interface contains options for a MessageResponse.

MessageResponseOptions.chain_of_thought.name: chain_of_thought
MessageResponseOptions.chain_of_thought.summary:
Controls the display of the chain of thought component.

Most people should use reasoning steps instead of chain of thought.

Chain of thought it meant more for technical "called X API and got Y result back".

Reasoning steps can include that kind of detail depending on your use case, but is meant more for user friendly
content than debugging technical internal content.

MessageResponseOptions.reasoning.name: reasoning
MessageResponseOptions.reasoning.summary:
Controls the display of the reasoning steps component.

Most people should use reasoning steps instead of chain of thought.

Chain of thought it meant more for technical "called X API and got Y result back".

Reasoning steps can include that kind of detail depending on your use case, but is meant more for user friendly
content than debugging technical internal content.

MessageResponseOptions.response_user_pro.name: response_user_profile
MessageResponseOptions.response_user_pro.summary: This is the profile for the human or assistant who sent or triggered this message.

MessageResponseTypes.name: MessageResponseTypes
MessageResponseTypes.summary: The set of possible message types in a response.

MessageResponseTypes.AUDIO.name: AUDIO
MessageResponseTypes.AUDIO.summary: Displays an audio clip to the user using an audio player.

MessageResponseTypes.BUTTON.name: BUTTON
MessageResponseTypes.BUTTON.summary: Displays a button that can either send a message back to the backend, open a url, or throw a client side event.

MessageResponseTypes.CARD.name: CARD
MessageResponseTypes.CARD.summary: Displays a card that can contain other response types.

MessageResponseTypes.CAROUSEL.name: CAROUSEL
MessageResponseTypes.CAROUSEL.summary: Displays a carousel of cards that can contain other response types.

MessageResponseTypes.CONNECT_TO_HUMAN_AG.name: CONNECT_TO_HUMAN_AGENT
MessageResponseTypes.CONNECT_TO_HUMAN_AG.summary: Indicates that the conversation should be escalated to a human agent and offers that opportunity to the user.

MessageResponseTypes.CONVERSATIONAL_SEAR.name: CONVERSATIONAL_SEARCH
MessageResponseTypes.CONVERSATIONAL_SEAR.summary: Ability to show citations on your RAG result.

MessageResponseTypes.DATE.name: DATE
MessageResponseTypes.DATE.summary: Asks the user to provide a date. This may result in a date picker being presented to the user.

MessageResponseTypes.GRID.name: GRID
MessageResponseTypes.GRID.summary: Ability to layout response types inside a grid.

MessageResponseTypes.IFRAME.name: IFRAME
MessageResponseTypes.IFRAME.summary: Displays the contents of an iframe to the user.

MessageResponseTypes.IMAGE.name: IMAGE
MessageResponseTypes.IMAGE.summary: Displays an image to the user.

MessageResponseTypes.INLINE_ERROR.name: INLINE_ERROR
MessageResponseTypes.INLINE_ERROR.summary: Displays a general error message to the user and include developer info to be logged and to debug.

MessageResponseTypes.OPTION.name: OPTION
MessageResponseTypes.OPTION.summary:
A response that requests the user choose an option from a list. The list of options may be presented as a list
of buttons or it may be from a drop-down.

MessageResponseTypes.PAUSE.name: PAUSE
MessageResponseTypes.PAUSE.summary:
Indicates that the chat should display a pause at this point in the conversation before displaying additional
items.

MessageResponseTypes.PREVIEW_CARD.name: PREVIEW_CARD
MessageResponseTypes.PREVIEW_CARD.summary: Displays a preview card that can take the user flow to a workspace view.

MessageResponseTypes.SYSTEM.name: SYSTEM
MessageResponseTypes.SYSTEM.summary:
Displays a system message to the user. System messages appear centered between other messages
and are styled differently to indicate they are not from the user or assistant.

If a response contains ONLY system messages, they render standalone (no avatar, no bubble).
If mixed with other response types, system messages render inline within the message bubble.

MessageResponseTypes.TEXT.name: TEXT
MessageResponseTypes.TEXT.summary: Represents a basic text response. The given text may contain rich content such as markdown.

MessageResponseTypes.USER_DEFINED.name: USER_DEFINED
MessageResponseTypes.USER_DEFINED.summary: A user defined response will be displayed according to custom logic in the client.

MessageResponseTypes.VIDEO.name: VIDEO
MessageResponseTypes.VIDEO.summary: Displays a video to the user using a video player.

MessageSendSource.name: MessageSendSource
MessageSendSource.summary: The different sources that can cause a send event to occur.

MessageSendSource.DATE_PICKER.name: DATE_PICKER
MessageSendSource.DATE_PICKER.summary: The user chose a value from the date picker.

MessageSendSource.EVENT.name: EVENT
MessageSendSource.EVENT.summary: This is used for message events.

MessageSendSource.HISTORY_UPDATE.name: HISTORY_UPDATE
MessageSendSource.HISTORY_UPDATE.summary: The message was sent as an event history update.

MessageSendSource.HOME_SCREEN_INPUT.name: HOME_SCREEN_INPUT
MessageSendSource.HOME_SCREEN_INPUT.summary: The user has entered a value from the input on the home screen.

MessageSendSource.HOME_SCREEN_STARTER.name: HOME_SCREEN_STARTER
MessageSendSource.HOME_SCREEN_STARTER.summary: The user clicked a starter from the home screen.

MessageSendSource.INSTANCE_SEND.name: INSTANCE_SEND
MessageSendSource.INSTANCE_SEND.summary: An external call to the public "instance.send" method was made.

MessageSendSource.MESSAGE_INPUT.name: MESSAGE_INPUT
MessageSendSource.MESSAGE_INPUT.summary: The user has entered a value from the main input on the message list.

MessageSendSource.OPTION_BUTTON.name: OPTION_BUTTON
MessageSendSource.OPTION_BUTTON.summary: The user clicked a button from an option response.

MessageSendSource.OPTION_DROP_DOWN.name: OPTION_DROP_DOWN
MessageSendSource.OPTION_DROP_DOWN.summary: The user selected a value from a dropdown for an option response.

MessageSendSource.POST_BACK_BUTTON.name: POST_BACK_BUTTON
MessageSendSource.POST_BACK_BUTTON.summary: The user clicked a post back button from a button response.

MessageSendSource.WELCOME_REQUEST.name: WELCOME_REQUEST
MessageSendSource.WELCOME_REQUEST.summary: A default request for the welcome message was made.

MessageState.name: MessageState
MessageState.summary:
Lifecycle state passed to ChatInstanceMessaging.upsertMessage to describe the
message's state after the upsert completes. Carbon AI Chat tracks this state internally;
it is never written onto a MessageResponse.

`addMessage`, `addMessageChunk`, and `upsertMessage` may all target the same message
id without producing duplicate `pre:receive` / `receive` events — Carbon AI Chat tracks
the recorded state per id and fires those events only on the first transition to
COMPLETE.

MessageState.COMPLETE.name: COMPLETE
MessageState.COMPLETE.summary:
The message has reached its final shape. Carbon AI Chat fires
BusEventType.PRE_RECEIVE and BusEventType.RECEIVE when a message
transitions into this state from any other state, including the case where no
message with this ID previously existed.

MessageState.ERROR.name: ERROR
MessageState.ERROR.summary:
The message terminated in an error condition. The chat displays the message as-is
and does not fire BusEventType.PRE_RECEIVE or BusEventType.RECEIVE
when a message transitions into this state. Treat `ERROR` as terminal — subsequent
upserts targeting the same id are still accepted but should be rare.

MessageState.STREAMING.name: STREAMING
MessageState.STREAMING.summary:
The message is still being constructed and further updates are expected. The
"stop streaming" affordance remains available while a message is in this state if
any item carries `streaming_metadata.cancellable: true`.

MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_DOWN.name: SIDE_PANEL_DOWN
MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_DOWN.summary: This shows an icon that indicates that the Carbon AI Chat can be collapsed into a side panel.

MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_LEFT.name: SIDE_PANEL_LEFT
MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_LEFT.summary: This shows an icon that indicates that the Carbon AI Chat can be collapsed into a side panel.

MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_RIGHT.name: SIDE_PANEL_RIGHT
MinimizeButtonIconType.SIDE_PANEL_RIGHT.summary: This shows an icon that indicates that the Carbon AI Chat can be collapsed into a side panel.

ObjectMap.name: ObjectMap
ObjectMap.summary:
This interface represents an object which behaves like a map. The object contains a set of properties representing
keys in the map and the values of those properties are all of the same type (TPropertyType). The type of the keys
defaults to any string but you can specify a type that is a string enum instead if you want a map that contains
only keys for a given enum (or other similar type).

OnErrorData.name: OnErrorData
OnErrorData.summary: Fired when a serious error in the chat occurs.

OnErrorData.catastrophicErrorType.name: catastrophicErrorType
OnErrorData.catastrophicErrorType.summary: If the error is of the severity that requires a whole restart of Carbon AI Chat.

OnErrorData.errorType.name: errorType
OnErrorData.errorType.summary: The type of error that occurred.

OnErrorData.message.name: message
OnErrorData.message.summary: A message associated with the error.

OnErrorData.otherData.name: otherData
OnErrorData.otherData.summary: An extra blob of data associated with the error. This may be a stack trace for thrown errors.

OnErrorType.name: OnErrorType
OnErrorType.summary: The different categories of errors that the system can record. These values are published for end user consumption.

OnErrorType.HYDRATION.name: HYDRATION
OnErrorType.HYDRATION.summary:
This indicates that some error occurred while trying to hydrate the chat. This will prevent the chat from
functioning.

OnErrorType.INTEGRATION_ERROR.name: INTEGRATION_ERROR
OnErrorType.INTEGRATION_ERROR.summary:
This indicates a known error with the configuration for a service desk. Fired when a connect_to_agent
response type is received, but none is configured.

OnErrorType.MESSAGE_COMMUNICATION.name: MESSAGE_COMMUNICATION
OnErrorType.MESSAGE_COMMUNICATION.summary:
Indicates an error sending a message to the assistant. This error is only generated after all retries have
failed and the system has given up.

OnErrorType.RENDER.name: RENDER
OnErrorType.RENDER.summary: This indicates an error in one of the components that occurs as part of rendering the UI.

OptionItem.name: OptionItem
OptionItem.summary:
An option item returned in a message response from an assistant. This response type is used when displaying a list
of options to the user. How the options are displayed is up to the client but is often displayed in either a
drop-down or as a list of buttons.

OptionItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
OptionItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

OptionItem.description.name: description
OptionItem.description.summary: An optional description to be shown alongside the options.

OptionItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
OptionItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

OptionItem.options.name: options
OptionItem.options.summary: An array of objects describing the options from which the user can choose.

OptionItem.preference.name: preference
OptionItem.preference.summary: The preferred type of control to display (e.g. button or dropdown).

OptionItem.response_type.name: response_type
OptionItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

OptionItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
OptionItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

OptionItem.title.name: title
OptionItem.title.summary: An optional title to be shown alongside the options.

OptionItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
OptionItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

OptionItemPreference.name: OptionItemPreference
OptionItemPreference.summary: The set of possible response preferences for an options response.

OptionItemPreference.BUTTON.name: BUTTON
OptionItemPreference.BUTTON.summary: Indicates the options should be displayed as buttons.

OptionItemPreference.DROPDOWN.name: DROPDOWN
OptionItemPreference.DROPDOWN.summary: Indicates the options should be displayed as a drop-down.

PageObjectId.name: PageObjectId
PageObjectId.summary:
An enum of all of our data-testid we use. For some elements (like INPUT) they can appear in multiple "panels"
(e.g. on the home screen and in the main chat window). There are provided testids for "panels" as well so you
can first select a panel and then select the correct child.

This extends the base PageObjectId from @carbon/ai-chat-components to include application-level test IDs.

PageObjectId.BUTTON_RESPONSE_PANEL.name: BUTTON_RESPONSE_PANEL
PageObjectId.BUTTON_RESPONSE_PANEL.summary: A panel that opens from a button response.

PageObjectId.CHAT_WIDGET.name: CHAT_WIDGET
PageObjectId.CHAT_WIDGET.summary: The root chat widget container (for scoping tests/accessibility checks).

PageObjectId.LAUNCHER.name: LAUNCHER
PageObjectId.LAUNCHER.summary: The launcher button to open the chat. This id is maintained across desktop and mobile launchers.

PanelType.name: PanelType
PanelType.summary: Describes the different panel types that Carbon AI Chat supports.

PanelType.DEFAULT.name: DEFAULT
PanelType.DEFAULT.summary: Opens the panel so that it overlays the main chat content.

PanelType.HISTORY.name: HISTORY
PanelType.HISTORY.summary:
Opens the history panel.

The history panel only appears in the chat panel when
config.history.isMobile is true.

PanelType.WORKSPACE.name: WORKSPACE
PanelType.WORKSPACE.summary:
Opens the panel in the Workspace layout.

On large screens, the panel is placed at the `preferredLocation` (`start` or `end`)
and pushes the chat content.

On small screens, the panel behaves like `DEFAULT`.

PartialItemChunk.name: PartialItemChunk
PartialItemChunk.summary: The interface for a chunk that represents a partial update (or first time chunk) to a message item.

PartialItemChunk.partial_item.name: partial_item
PartialItemChunk.partial_item.summary:
The partial details of the item. The client will decide what rules to follow for merging this in with any
existing data for the same item (which is identified using the ItemStreamingMetadata.id property).

PartialItemChunk.partial_response.name: partial_response
PartialItemChunk.partial_response.summary: Change the agent display name and other items on the full response.

PartialItemChunk.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
PartialItemChunk.streaming_metadata.summary: Additional metadata associated with the stream.

PartialItemChunkWithId.name: PartialItemChunkWithId
PartialItemChunkWithId.summary:
A stricter partial item chunk type for streaming implementations that want compile-time enforcement of
ItemStreamingMetadata.id. This is optional and not required for compatibility.
PartialItemChunkWithId.signature: type PartialItemChunkWithId = Omit<PartialItemChunk, "partial_item"> & { partial_item: DeepPartial<GenericItem> & { streaming_metadata: ItemStreamingMetadata } }

PartialResponse.message_options.name: message_options
PartialResponse.message_options.summary: This contains the history of this response.

PauseItem.name: PauseItem
PauseItem.summary:
A pause item returned in a message response from an assistant. This indicates that the client should pause before
displaying additional response items.

PauseItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
PauseItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

PauseItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
PauseItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

PauseItem.response_type.name: response_type
PauseItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

PauseItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
PauseItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

PauseItem.time.name: time
PauseItem.time.summary: How long to pause, in milliseconds.

PauseItem.typing.name: typing
PauseItem.typing.summary: Whether to display an "is typing" indicator during the pause.

PauseItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
PauseItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

PerCornerConfig.name: PerCornerConfig
PerCornerConfig.summary:
Configuration for individual corners using logical property names.
Supports RTL layouts by using start/end instead of left/right.

Any undefined corner will fall back to the default value (ROUND).

PerCornerConfig.endEnd.name: endEnd
PerCornerConfig.endEnd.summary:
Bottom-right corner in LTR, bottom-left in RTL.
Maps to border-end-end-radius.

PerCornerConfig.endStart.name: endStart
PerCornerConfig.endStart.summary:
Bottom-left corner in LTR, bottom-right in RTL.
Maps to border-end-start-radius.

PerCornerConfig.startEnd.name: startEnd
PerCornerConfig.startEnd.summary:
Top-right corner in LTR, top-left in RTL.
Maps to border-start-end-radius.

PerCornerConfig.startStart.name: startStart
PerCornerConfig.startStart.summary:
Top-left corner in LTR, top-right in RTL.
Maps to border-start-start-radius.

PersistedHumanAgentState.name: PersistedHumanAgentState
PersistedHumanAgentState.summary: The subset of HumanAgentState that is persisted to browser storage.

PersistedHumanAgentState.isConnected.name: isConnected
PersistedHumanAgentState.isConnected.summary: Indicates that the user is connected to a human agent.

PersistedHumanAgentState.isSuspended.name: isSuspended
PersistedHumanAgentState.isSuspended.summary: Indicates if the human agent conversation is currently suspended.

PersistedHumanAgentState.responseUserPro.name: responseUserProfile
PersistedHumanAgentState.responseUserPro.summary: The profile of the last human agent to join the chat.

PersistedHumanAgentState.responseUserPro.name: responseUserProfiles
PersistedHumanAgentState.responseUserPro.summary: Cache of known agent profiles by ID.

PersistedHumanAgentState.serviceDeskStat.name: serviceDeskState
PersistedHumanAgentState.serviceDeskStat.summary: Arbitrary state saved by the service desk.

PersistedState.disclaimersAccepted.name: disclaimersAccepted
PersistedState.disclaimersAccepted.summary:
Map of if a disclaimer has been accepted on a given window.hostname value, keyed by hostname via
ObjectMap.

PersistedState.hasSentNonWelcomeMessage.name: hasSentNonWelcomeMessage
PersistedState.hasSentNonWelcomeMessage.summary:
If the user has received a message beyond the welcome node. We use this to mark if the chat has been interacted
with. This flag is duplicated so the information is available before hydration and before the user is known.
Note that this property reflects only the last user and should only be used when an approximate value is
acceptable.

PersistedState.humanAgentState.name: humanAgentState
PersistedState.humanAgentState.summary: The persisted subset of the human agent state.

PersistedState.launcherIsExpanded.name: launcherIsExpanded
PersistedState.launcherIsExpanded.summary: Indicates if the launcher should be in the expanded state.

PersistedState.launcherShouldStartCallTo.name: launcherShouldStartCallToActionCounterIfEnabled
PersistedState.launcherShouldStartCallTo.summary: Determines if the launcher should start a timer to show its expanded state.

PersistedState.showUnreadIndicator.name: showUnreadIndicator
PersistedState.showUnreadIndicator.summary:
Indicates if we should show an unread indicator on the launcher. This is set by
ChatInstance.updateAssistantUnreadIndicatorVisibility and will display an empty circle on
the launcher. This setting is overridden if there are any unread human agent messages in which case a circle
with a number is displayed.

PersistedState.version.name: version
PersistedState.version.summary:
The version of the Carbon AI Chat that this data is persisted for. If there are any breaking changes to the
application state and a user reloads and gets a new version of the widget, bad things might happen so we'll
just invalidate the persisted storage if we ever attempt to load an old version on Carbon AI Chat startup.

PersistedState.viewState.name: viewState
PersistedState.viewState.summary: Indicates which of the Carbon AI Chat views are visible and which are hidden.

PersistedState.wasLoadedFromBrowser.name: wasLoadedFromBrowser
PersistedState.wasLoadedFromBrowser.summary: Indicates if this state was loaded from browser session storage or if was created as part of a new session.

PreviewCardItem.name: PreviewCardItem
PreviewCardItem.summary: This message item represents a preview card that can trigger a workflow view.

PreviewCardItem.additional_data.name: additional_data
PreviewCardItem.additional_data.summary: Additional data to be passed to workspace.

PreviewCardItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
PreviewCardItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

PreviewCardItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
PreviewCardItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

PreviewCardItem.response_type.name: response_type
PreviewCardItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

PreviewCardItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
PreviewCardItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

PreviewCardItem.subtitle.name: subtitle
PreviewCardItem.subtitle.summary: The subtitle of the preview card.

PreviewCardItem.title.name: title
PreviewCardItem.title.summary: The title of the preview card.

PreviewCardItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
PreviewCardItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

PreviewCardItem.workspace_id.name: workspace_id
PreviewCardItem.workspace_id.summary: The id of the workspace that is attached to this card.

PublicChatHumanAgentState.name: PublicChatHumanAgentState
PublicChatHumanAgentState.summary: Current connection state of the human agent experience.

PublicChatState.name: PublicChatState
PublicChatState.signature: type PublicChatState = Readonly<Omit<PersistedState, "humanAgentState"> & { activeResponseId: string | null; customPanels: PublicCustomPanelsState; humanAgent: PublicChatHumanAgentState; input: PublicInputState; isHydratingCounter: number; isMessageLoadingCounter: number; isMessageLoadingText?: string; workspace: PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState }>

PublicConfig.name: PublicConfig
PublicConfig.summary: Configuration interface for Carbon AI Chat.

PublicConfig.aiEnabled.name: aiEnabled
PublicConfig.aiEnabled.summary: Enables Carbon AI theme styling. Defaults to true.

PublicConfig.assistantAvatarUrl.name: assistantAvatarUrl
PublicConfig.assistantAvatarUrl.summary: Sets the URL pointing to a custom avatar for the response author. This image should be a square. If not provided, the default Watsonx icon will be used.

PublicConfig.assistantName.name: assistantName
PublicConfig.assistantName.summary: Sets the name of the assistant. Defaults to "watsonx". Used in screen reader announcements and error messages.

PublicConfig.debug.name: debug
PublicConfig.debug.summary: Add a bunch of noisy console.log messages!

PublicConfig.disableCustomElementMobileE.name: disableCustomElementMobileEnhancements
PublicConfig.disableCustomElementMobileE.summary:
This value is only used when a custom element is being used to render the widget. By default, a number of
enhancements to the widget are activated on mobile devices which can interfere with a custom element. This
value can be used to disable those enhancements while using a custom element.

PublicConfig.disclaimer.name: disclaimer
PublicConfig.disclaimer.summary:
Disclaimer screen configuration.

If `disclaimerHTML` changes after the disclaimer has been accepted, we request a user to accept again.

PublicConfig.header.name: header
PublicConfig.header.summary: Extra config for controlling the behavior of the header.

PublicConfig.history.name: history
PublicConfig.history.summary: The config object for chat history.

PublicConfig.homescreen.name: homescreen
PublicConfig.homescreen.summary:
Configuration for the homescreen.

If you change anything but `is_on` after the chat session has started, the chat will handle it gracefully.

If you turn on the homescreen after the user has already started chatting, it will show up in the header as
an icon, but the user won't be forced to go back to the homescreen (unlike turning on the disclaimer mid-chat).

PublicConfig.injectCarbonTheme.name: injectCarbonTheme
PublicConfig.injectCarbonTheme.summary:
Which Carbon theme tokens to inject. If unset (falsy), the chat inherits tokens from the host page.
Set to a specific theme to force token injection.

PublicConfig.input.name: input
PublicConfig.input.summary: Configuration for the main input field on the chat.

PublicConfig.isReadonly.name: isReadonly
PublicConfig.isReadonly.summary: Sets the chat into a read only mode for displaying old conversations.

PublicConfig.keyboardShortcuts.name: keyboardShortcuts
PublicConfig.keyboardShortcuts.summary:
Configuration for keyboard shortcuts in the chat.
Allows customization of keyboard shortcuts for various actions.

PublicConfig.layout.name: layout
PublicConfig.layout.summary: The config object for changing Carbon AI Chat's layout.

PublicConfig.locale.name: locale
PublicConfig.locale.summary:
The locale to use for the widget. This controls the language pack and regional formatting.
Example values include: 'en', 'en-us', 'fr', 'es'.

PublicConfig.markdown.name: markdown
PublicConfig.markdown.summary:
Markdown rendering customization. The framework-neutral subset; React and
web-component layers extend this with their own `customRenderers` member.

PublicConfig.namespace.name: namespace
PublicConfig.namespace.summary:
An optional namespace that can be added to the Carbon AI Chat that must be 30 characters or under. This value is
intended to enable multiple instances of the Carbon AI Chat to be used on the same page. The namespace for this web
chat. This value is used to generate a value to append to anything unique (id, session keys, etc) to allow
multiple Carbon AI Chats on the same page.

Note: this value is used in the aria region label for the Carbon AI Chat. This means this value will be read out loud
by users using a screen reader.

PublicConfig.onError.name: onError
PublicConfig.onError.summary:
This is a one-off listener for catastrophic errors. This is used instead of a normal event bus handler because this function can be
defined and called before the event bus has been created.

PublicConfig.openChatByDefault.name: openChatByDefault
PublicConfig.openChatByDefault.summary: By default, the chat window will be rendered in a "closed" state.

PublicConfig.persistFeedback.name: persistFeedback
PublicConfig.persistFeedback.summary: Allows for feedback to persist in all messages, not just the latest message.

PublicConfig.serviceDesk.name: serviceDesk
PublicConfig.serviceDesk.summary: Any public config to apply to service desks.

PublicConfig.serviceDeskFactory.name: serviceDeskFactory
PublicConfig.serviceDeskFactory.summary:
This is a factory for producing custom implementations of service desks. If this value is set, then this will
be used to create an instance of a ServiceDesk when the user attempts to connect to an agent.

If it is changed in the middle of a conversation (you should obviously avoid this) the conversation with the
human agent will be disconnected.

PublicConfig.shouldSanitizeHTML.name: shouldSanitizeHTML
PublicConfig.shouldSanitizeHTML.summary: Indicates if Carbon AI Chat should sanitize HTML from the assistant.

PublicConfig.shouldTakeFocusIfOpensAutom.name: shouldTakeFocusIfOpensAutomatically
PublicConfig.shouldTakeFocusIfOpensAutom.summary: If the Carbon AI Chat should grab focus if the chat is open on page load.

PublicConfig.strings.name: strings
PublicConfig.strings.summary: Optional partial language pack overrides. Values merge with defaults.

PublicConfig.upload.name: upload
PublicConfig.upload.summary:
Configuration for file upload behavior in the chat input.
When `is_on` is `true`, the chat renders a file attachment button in the input area.

PublicConfigMarkdown.name: PublicConfigMarkdown
PublicConfigMarkdown.summary:
Framework-neutral markdown configuration shared by the React `ChatContainer`
and the `cds-aichat-container` web component. Each layer extends this with
its own `customRenderers` member returning the layer-appropriate type
(`ReactNode` vs `HTMLElement | null`).

PublicConfigMarkdown.markdownItPlugins.name: markdownItPlugins
PublicConfigMarkdown.markdownItPlugins.summary:
Markdown-it plugins applied after the built-in plugins
(markdown-it-attrs, markdown-it-highlight, markdown-it-task-lists).
Memoize this array — a new reference each render rebuilds the
markdown-it instance.

PublicConfigMessaging.customLoadHistory.name: customLoadHistory
PublicConfigMessaging.customLoadHistory.summary:
This is a callback function that is used by Carbon AI Chat to retrieve history data for populating the Carbon AI Chat. If
this function is defined, it will be used instead of any other mechanism for fetching history.

If this function is mutated after it was initially called, the chat does not re-call it.

PublicConfigMessaging.customSendMessage.name: customSendMessage
PublicConfigMessaging.customSendMessage.summary:
A callback for Carbon AI Chat to use to send messages to your assistant.

Carbon AI Chat will queue up any additional user messages until the Promise from a previous call to customSendMessage
has resolved. If you do not make customSendMessage async, it will be up to you to manage what happens when a message is
sent when the previous is still processing. If the Promise rejects, an error indicator will be displayed next to the user's message.

If the request takes longer than PublicConfigMessaging.messageTimeoutSecs than the AbortSignal will be sent.
PublicConfigMessaging.customSendMessage.signature: customSendMessage?: (request: MessageRequest, requestOptions: CustomSendMessageOptions, instance: ChatInstance) => void | Promise<void>

PublicConfigMessaging.messageLoadingIndi.name: messageLoadingIndicatorTimeoutSecs
PublicConfigMessaging.messageLoadingIndi.summary:
Controls how long AI chat should wait before showing the loading indicator. If set to 0, the chat will never show
the loading indicator on its own. This is tied to either ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessage or
ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessageChunk being called after this message was sent. If neither of those methods
are called with in the window defined here, the loading indicator will be shown.

PublicConfigMessaging.messageTimeoutSecs.name: messageTimeoutSecs
PublicConfigMessaging.messageTimeoutSecs.summary:
Changes the timeout used by the message service when making message calls. The timeout is in seconds. The
default is 150 seconds. After this time, an error will be shown in the client and an Abort signal will be sent
to customSendMessage. If set to 0, the chat will never timeout.  This is tied to either ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessage or
ChatInstanceMessaging.addMessageChunk being called after this message was sent. If neither of those methods
are called with in the window defined here, the chat will timeout (unless the value is set to 0).

PublicConfigMessaging.showStopButtonImme.name: showStopButtonImmediately
PublicConfigMessaging.showStopButtonImme.summary:
Controls when the stop streaming button becomes visible during message streaming.

You must have PublicConfigMessaging.customSendMessage return a promise for
this setting to work correctly.

When `true`, the stop button appears immediately when `customSendMessage` is called,
allowing users to cancel requests before the first streaming chunk arrives. This is
useful for slow-starting requests or when you want to give users immediate control
over long-running operations. The button will remain visible as long as there is an
active streaming message, even if the initial message promise resolves.

When `false` (default), the stop button only appears after the first streaming chunk
arrives with `cancellable: true` metadata, maintaining backward compatibility with
existing behavior.

PublicConfigMessaging.skipWelcome.name: skipWelcome
PublicConfigMessaging.skipWelcome.summary:
Indicates if Carbon AI Chat should make a request for the welcome message when a new conversation begins. If this is
true, then Carbon AI Chat will start with an empty conversation.

**Manual session management required**: Changes to this property after conversation has started have no effect.
To apply new welcome behavior, call `instance.messaging.restartConversation()`.

PublicCustomPanelsState.name: PublicCustomPanelsState
PublicCustomPanelsState.summary: Represents public state for each supported custom panel variant.

PublicCustomPanelsState.default.name: default
PublicCustomPanelsState.default.summary: State for the default overlay-style custom panel.

PublicCustomPanelsState.workspace.name: workspace
PublicCustomPanelsState.workspace.summary: State for the workspace custom panel.

PublicDefaultCustomPanelState.name: PublicDefaultCustomPanelState
PublicDefaultCustomPanelState.summary: Represents public state for default custom panel.

PublicDefaultCustomPanelState.isOpen.name: isOpen
