PublicDefaultCustomPanelState.isOpen.summary: Indicates if the default custom panel overlay is currently open.

PublicHistoryPanelState.name: PublicHistoryPanelState
PublicHistoryPanelState.summary: Represents public state for history panel.

PublicHistoryPanelState.isMobile.name: isMobile
PublicHistoryPanelState.isMobile.summary: Indicates if the history panel should open in chat panel.

PublicHistoryPanelState.isOpen.name: isOpen
PublicHistoryPanelState.isOpen.summary: Indicates if the history panel is currently open.

PublicInputState.name: PublicInputState
PublicInputState.summary: This is the state made available by calling ChatInstance.getState. This is a public method that returns immutable values.

PublicInputState.hasInFlightUploads.name: hasInFlightUploads
PublicInputState.hasInFlightUploads.summary:
`true` while one or more file uploads initiated via UploadConfig.onFileUpload are still
in progress.  The send button is disabled while this is `true`.

PublicInputState.rawValue.name: rawValue
PublicInputState.rawValue.summary: Raw text currently queued in the input before being sent to customSendMessage.

PublicInputState.structuredData.name: structuredData
PublicInputState.structuredData.summary:
A snapshot of the pending structured data currently queued in the input. This data will be merged
into the next outgoing MessageRequest when the user sends a message via the UI.

PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.name: PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState
PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.summary: Represents public state for workspace custom panel.

PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.addition.name: additionalData
PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.addition.summary: Additional metadata associated with the workspace.

PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.isOpen.name: isOpen
PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.isOpen.summary: Indicates if the workspace custom panel overlay is currently open.

PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.options.name: options
PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.options.summary: Config options for the workspace panels.

PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.workspac.name: workspaceID
PublicWorkspaceCustomPanelState.workspac.summary: The ID of the workspace attached to this panel. Used to match with a given Preview Card.

ReasoningStep.content.name: content
ReasoningStep.content.summary:
Optional markdown content (string) or a list of GenericItem response items to render inside this
reasoning step. When an array is supplied, each item is rendered through the standard message renderer.

GenericItem variants that render purely from their own item data (TextItem, ImageItem, UserDefinedItem,
ButtonItem, InlineErrorItem, etc.) are fully supported. Variants whose renderers rely on nested-body
hydration (Card, Carousel, Grid bodies) will not hydrate those nested children when used here.

ReasoningStep.open_state.name: open_state
ReasoningStep.open_state.summary:
Marks if this individual step is open. Only use this if you don't want the default behavior.

If the step has content, by default the reasoning step will automatically open and will close when the
next step(s) have content or the first GenericItem is returned with something to display.

No matter what you choose, if the user manually marks something open/closed they retain control.

ReasoningStep.title.name: title
ReasoningStep.title.summary: The title of the reasoning step.

ReasoningStepOpenState.name: ReasoningStepOpenState
ReasoningStepOpenState.summary:
If the reasoning step is open, closed, or is controlled by Carbon AI Chat.

If a user elects to open/close the user action will override what is provided here.

ReasoningSteps.name: ReasoningSteps
ReasoningSteps.summary: The interface describing how to pass reasoning steps to the UI.

ReasoningSteps.content.name: content
ReasoningSteps.content.summary:
Optional markdown content (string) or a list of GenericItem response items to render as a preamble
above the reasoning steps. When an array is supplied, each item is rendered through the standard message
renderer.

GenericItem variants that render purely from their own item data (TextItem, ImageItem, UserDefinedItem,
ButtonItem, InlineErrorItem, etc.) are fully supported. Variants whose renderers rely on nested-body
hydration (Card, Carousel, Grid bodies) will not hydrate those nested children when used here.

ReasoningSteps.open_state.name: open_state
ReasoningSteps.open_state.summary:
Marks if the reasoning step interface is open. Only use this if you don't want the default behavior.

By default the reasoning step interface will automatically open and will then close when the first
GenericItem is returned with something to display.

No matter what you choose, if the user manually marks something open/closed they retain control.

ReasoningSteps.steps.name: steps
ReasoningSteps.steps.summary: The array of reasoning steps for this message.

RenderCustomMessageFooter.name: RenderCustomMessageFooter
RenderCustomMessageFooter.summary:
The type of the render function that is used to render a custom footer. This function should return a
component that renders the custom message footer.
RenderCustomMessageFooter.signature: type RenderCustomMessageFooter = (slotName: string, message: MessageResponse, messageItem: GenericItem, instance: ChatInstance, additionalData?: Record<string, unknown>) => ReactNode | null

RenderCustomMessageFooterState.name: RenderCustomMessageFooterState
RenderCustomMessageFooterState.summary:
The accumulated state for one custom message footer slot, passed to the
web component WCRenderCustomMessageFooter callback.

RenderCustomMessageFooterState.additiona.name: additionalData
RenderCustomMessageFooterState.additiona.summary: Optional application data supplied with the footer slot.

RenderCustomMessageFooterState.message.name: message
RenderCustomMessageFooterState.message.summary: The assistant response object that contains the messageItem.

RenderCustomMessageFooterState.messageIt.name: messageItem
RenderCustomMessageFooterState.messageIt.summary: The message item that the footer is attached to.

RenderCustomMessageFooterState.slotName.name: slotName
RenderCustomMessageFooterState.slotName.summary: The unique identifier for this footer slot.

RenderUserDefinedResponse.name: RenderUserDefinedResponse
RenderUserDefinedResponse.summary:
The type of the render function that is used to render user defined responses. This function should return a
component that renders the display for the message contained in the given event.
RenderUserDefinedResponse.signature: type RenderUserDefinedResponse = (state: RenderUserDefinedState, instance: ChatInstance) => ReactNode

RenderUserDefinedState.name: RenderUserDefinedState
RenderUserDefinedState.summary: The user_defined message object passed into the renderUserDefinedResponse property on the main chat components.

RenderUserDefinedState.fullMessage.name: fullMessage
RenderUserDefinedState.fullMessage.summary: The entire message object received when the entire message (not just the individual messageItem) has finished processing.

RenderUserDefinedState.messageItem.name: messageItem
RenderUserDefinedState.messageItem.summary:
The messageItem after all partial chunks are received. This will first be set to the value of the `complete_item`
chunk.
Once the fullMessage is resolved, this value will update to the value of the item in the fullMessage, which will
be the same value unless you have done any post-processing mutations.

RenderUserDefinedState.partialItems.name: partialItems
RenderUserDefinedState.partialItems.summary:
An array of each user defined item partial chunk. Each chunk contains the new chunk information, they are not
concatenated for you. When messageItem has been set an no more chunks are expected, this property is removed
to avoid memory leaks.

RenderUserDefinedState.state.name: state
RenderUserDefinedState.state.summary:
The current MessageState of the containing message at the moment the renderer
was invoked. Use this to drive in-widget streaming indicators or error treatments
without inspecting the message items directly.

 Field is additive; its presence and semantics may evolve as the
lifecycle model stabilizes.

RenderWriteableElementResponse.name: RenderWriteableElementResponse
RenderWriteableElementResponse.summary: A map of writeable element keys to a ReactNode to render to them.
RenderWriteableElementResponse.signature: type RenderWriteableElementResponse = { [K in keyof WriteableElements]?: ReactNode }

ResolvedCornerConfig.name: ResolvedCornerConfig
ResolvedCornerConfig.summary:
Resolved corner configuration with all corners defined.
Used internally after normalizing the user's configuration.

ResponseUserProfile.name: ResponseUserProfile
ResponseUserProfile.summary:
Profile information about a specific agent that can be used to display information to the user. This may
represent a human agent or a virtual assistant agent.

ResponseUserProfile.id.name: id
ResponseUserProfile.id.summary: A unique identifier for this agent.

ResponseUserProfile.nickname.name: nickname
ResponseUserProfile.nickname.summary: The visible name for the response author. Can be the full name or just a first name for a human.

ResponseUserProfile.profile_picture_url.name: profile_picture_url
ResponseUserProfile.profile_picture_url.summary: A URL pointing to an avatar for the response author. This image should be a square.

ResponseUserProfile.user_type.name: user_type
ResponseUserProfile.user_type.summary:
The type of user. If its a "human" there is more protection against code injection attacks, where as assistant responses
are trusted by default unless PublicConfig.shouldSanitizeHTML is set to true.

ScreenShareState.name: ScreenShareState
ScreenShareState.summary: The possible state changes for a screen sharing request.

ScreenShareState.ACCEPTED.name: ACCEPTED
ScreenShareState.ACCEPTED.summary: Indicates the screen sharing was accepted by the user.

ScreenShareState.CANCELLED.name: CANCELLED
ScreenShareState.CANCELLED.summary: Indicates the screen sharing request was cancelled.

ScreenShareState.DECLINED.name: DECLINED
ScreenShareState.DECLINED.summary: Indicates the screen sharing was declined by the user.

ScreenShareState.ENDED.name: ENDED
ScreenShareState.ENDED.summary: Indicates that screen sharing has ended.

SearchResult.body.name: body
SearchResult.body.summary: The search result. This can be drilled into and viewed in a larger and scrollable format.

SendOptions.name: SendOptions
SendOptions.summary: This interface represents the options for when a MessageRequest is sent to the server with the send method.

SendOptions.silent.name: silent
SendOptions.silent.summary:
If you want to send a message to the API, but NOT have it show up in the UI, set this to true. The "pre:send"
and "send" events will still be fired but the message will not be added to the local message list displayed in
the UI. Note that the response message will still be added.

ServiceDesk.name: ServiceDesk
ServiceDesk.summary:
This is the public interface for a human agent service desk integration. This is the interface between the chat
widget and the implementation of the human agent interface with one of the various supported service desks.

ServiceDesk.areAnyAgentsOnline.name: areAnyAgentsOnline
ServiceDesk.areAnyAgentsOnline.summary:
Checks if any agents are online and can connect to a user when they become available. This does not necessarily
mean that an agent is immediately available; when a chat is started, the user may still have to wait for an
agent to become available. The callback function ServiceDeskCallback.updateAgentAvailability is used to
give the user more up-to-date information while they are waiting for an agent to become available.

ServiceDesk.endChat.name: endChat
ServiceDesk.endChat.summary: Tells the service desk to terminate the chat.

ServiceDesk.filesSelectedForUpload.name: filesSelectedForUpload
ServiceDesk.filesSelectedForUpload.summary:
Indicates that the user has selected some files to be uploaded but that the user has not yet chosen to send
them to the agent. This method can use this as an opportunity to perform any early validation of the files in
order to display an error to the user. It should not actually upload the files at this point. If the user
chooses to send the files to the agent, they will be included later when ServiceDesk#sendMessageToAgent is called.

This method may be called multiple times before a user sends the files.

If there are errors in the files, this method should use ServiceDeskCallback#setFileUploadStatus to update
the status with an error message. The user will not be able to upload any files until any files in error are
removed.

ServiceDesk.getName.name: getName
ServiceDesk.getName.summary:
Returns a name for this service desk integration. This value should reflect the service desk that is being
integrated to (e.g. "genesys web messenger"). This information will be reported to IBM and may be used to gauge
interest in various service desks for the possibility of creating fully supported out-of-the-box implementations.

This value is required for custom service desks and may have a maximum of 40 characters.

ServiceDesk.reconnect.name: reconnect
ServiceDesk.reconnect.summary:
This will be called when the service desk is first initialized and it is determined that the user was previously
connected to an agent. This function should perform whatever steps are necessary to reconnect the user. Web chat
will assume the user is permitted to send messages and is connected to the same agent when this function resolves.

ServiceDesk.screenShareStop.name: screenShareStop
ServiceDesk.screenShareStop.summary: Tells the service desk that the user has requested to stop sharing their screen.

ServiceDesk.sendMessageToAgent.name: sendMessageToAgent
ServiceDesk.sendMessageToAgent.summary:
Sends a message to the agent in the service desk. Note that the message text may be empty if the user has
selected files to upload and has not chosen to include a message to go along with the files.
ServiceDesk.sendMessageToAgent.signature: sendMessageToAgent: (message: MessageRequest, messageID: string, additionalData: AdditionalDataToAgent) => Promise<void>

ServiceDesk.startChat.name: startChat
ServiceDesk.startChat.summary:
Instructs the service desk to start a new chat. This will be called when a user requests to connect to an agent
and Carbon AI Chat initiates the process (typically when the user clicks the button on the "Connect to Agent" card).
It will make the appropriate calls to the service desk to start the chat and will make use of the callback to
inform Carbon AI Chat when an agent joins or messages are received.

This may be called multiple times by Carbon AI Chat. If a user begins a chat with an agent, ends the chat and then
begins a new chat with an agent, this function will be called again.

If the integration is unable to start a chat (such as if the service desk is down or no agents are available)
then this function should throw an error to let Carbon AI Chat know that the chat could not be started.

The areAnyAgentsOnline function is called before this function is called and is called as soon as a
"connect_to_agent" message has been received from the assistant. This determines if the "Connect to Agent" card
should be displayed to the user or if the "no agents are available" message configured in the skill should be
shown instead.

ServiceDesk.userReadMessages.name: userReadMessages
ServiceDesk.userReadMessages.summary: Informs the service desk that the user has read all the messages that have been sent by the service desk.

ServiceDesk.userTyping.name: userTyping
ServiceDesk.userTyping.summary: Tells the service desk if a user has started or stopped typing.

ServiceDeskCallback.name: ServiceDeskCallback
ServiceDeskCallback.summary:
This interface represents the operations that a service desk integration can call on Carbon AI Chat when it wants web
chat to do something. When a service desk integration instance is created, Carbon AI Chat will provide an
implementation of this interface to the integration for it to use.

ServiceDeskCallback.agentEndedChat.name: agentEndedChat
ServiceDeskCallback.agentEndedChat.summary: Informs the chat widget that the agent has ended the conversation.

ServiceDeskCallback.agentJoined.name: agentJoined
ServiceDeskCallback.agentJoined.summary: Informs the chat widget that an agent has joined the chat.

ServiceDeskCallback.agentLeftChat.name: agentLeftChat
ServiceDeskCallback.agentLeftChat.summary:
Informs the chat widget that the agent has left the conversation. This does not end the conversation itself,
rather the only action that occurs is the visitor receives the agent left status message. If the user sends
another message, it is up to the service desk to decide what to do with it.

ServiceDeskCallback.agentReadMessages.name: agentReadMessages
ServiceDeskCallback.agentReadMessages.summary: Informs the chat widget that the agent has read all the messages that have been sent to the service desk.

ServiceDeskCallback.agentTyping.name: agentTyping
ServiceDeskCallback.agentTyping.summary: Tells the chat widget if an agent has started or stopped typing.

ServiceDeskCallback.beginTransferToAnoth.name: beginTransferToAnotherAgent
ServiceDeskCallback.beginTransferToAnoth.summary:
Informs the chat widget that a transfer to another agent is in progress. The agent profile information is
optional if the service desk doesn't have the information available. This message simply tells the chat widget
that the transfer has started. The service desk should inform the widget when the transfer is complete by
sending a agentJoined message later.

ServiceDeskCallback.persistedState.name: persistedState
ServiceDeskCallback.persistedState.summary:
Returns the persisted agent state from the store. This is the current state as updated by
updatePersistedState. The object returned here is frozen and may not be modified.

ServiceDeskCallback.screenShareEnded.name: screenShareEnded
ServiceDeskCallback.screenShareEnded.summary:
Informs Carbon AI Chat that a screen sharing session has ended or been cancelled. This may occur while waiting for a
screen sharing request to be accepted or while screen sharing is in progress.

ServiceDeskCallback.screenShareRequest.name: screenShareRequest
ServiceDeskCallback.screenShareRequest.summary:
Requests that the user share their screen with the agent. This will present a modal dialog to the user who must
respond before continuing the conversation. This method returns a Promise that resolves when the user has
responded to the request or the request times out.

ServiceDeskCallback.sendMessageToUser.name: sendMessageToUser
ServiceDeskCallback.sendMessageToUser.summary:
Sends a message to the chat widget from an agent.

Note: The text response type from the standard Watson API is supported in addition to the Carbon AI Chat specific
MessageResponseTypes.INLINE_ERROR response type.
ServiceDeskCallback.sendMessageToUser.signature: sendMessageToUser(message: string | MessageResponse<GenericItem<Record<string, unknown>>[]>, agentID?: string): Promise<void>

ServiceDeskCallback.setErrorStatus.name: setErrorStatus
ServiceDeskCallback.setErrorStatus.summary: Sets the state of the given error type.

ServiceDeskCallback.setFileUploadStatus.name: setFileUploadStatus
ServiceDeskCallback.setFileUploadStatus.summary:
Updates the status of a file upload. The upload may either be successful or an error may have occurred. The
location of a file upload may be in one of two places. The first occurs when the user has selected a file to be
uploaded but has not yet sent the file. In this case, the file appears inside the Carbon AI Chat input area. If an
error is indicated on the file, the error message will be displayed along with the file and the user must
remove the file from the input area before a message can be sent.

The second occurs after the user has sent the file and the service desk has begun to upload the file. In this
case, the file no longer appears in the input area but appears as a sent message in the message list. If an
error occurs during this time, an icon will appear next to the message to indicate an error occurred and an
error message will be added to the message list.
ServiceDeskCallback.setFileUploadStatus.signature: setFileUploadStatus(fileID: string, isError?: boolean, errorMessage?: string): Promise<void>

ServiceDeskCallback.updateAgentAvailabil.name: updateAgentAvailability
ServiceDeskCallback.updateAgentAvailabil.summary:
Sends updated availability information to the chat widget for a user who is waiting to be connected to an
agent (e.g. the user is number 2 in line). This may be called at any point while waiting for the connection to
provide newer information.

Note: Of the fields in the AgentAvailability object, only one of positionInQueue and estimatedWaitTime can be
rendered in the widget. If both fields are provided, estimatedWaitTime will take priority and the
positionInQueue field will be ignored.

ServiceDeskCallback.updateCapabilities.name: updateCapabilities
ServiceDeskCallback.updateCapabilities.summary:
Updates Carbon AI Chat with the capabilities supported by the service desk. Some of these capabilities may support
being changed dynamically and can be updated at any time.

ServiceDeskCallback.updatePersistedState.name: updatePersistedState
ServiceDeskCallback.updatePersistedState.summary:
Allows the service desk to store state that may be retrieved when Carbon AI Chat is reloaded on a page. This information
is stored in browser session storage which has a total limit of 5MB per origin so the storage should be used
sparingly. Also, the value provided here must be JSON serializable.

When Carbon AI Chat is reloaded, the data provided here will be returned to the service desk via the
ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.persistedState property. This data may also be retrieved by using the
persistedState method.

ServiceDeskCapabilities.name: ServiceDeskCapabilities
ServiceDeskCapabilities.summary: The set of capabilities and parameters that are supported by the service desk.

ServiceDeskErrorInfo.name: ServiceDeskErrorInfo
ServiceDeskErrorInfo.summary:
The type for the information passed to ServiceDeskCallback#setErrorStatus. It is a discriminating union
where the type property is the discriminating value that determines which child interface is to be used.

ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.name: ServiceDeskFactoryParameters
ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.summary: The parameters that are passed to a service desk factory.

ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.callback.name: callback
ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.callback.summary: The callback used by the service desk to communicate with the widget.

ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.instance.name: instance
ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.instance.summary: The instance of Carbon AI Chat.

ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.persistedSt.name: persistedState
ServiceDeskFactoryParameters.persistedSt.summary: Any state that was stored for the service desk. This value may be undefined if no state has been stored.

ServiceDeskPublicConfig.name: ServiceDeskPublicConfig
ServiceDeskPublicConfig.summary: The section of the public config that contains configuration options for service desk integrations.

ServiceDeskPublicConfig.agentJoinTimeout.name: agentJoinTimeoutSeconds
ServiceDeskPublicConfig.agentJoinTimeout.summary:
The timeout value is seconds to use when waiting for an agent to join the chat after an agent has been
requested. If no agent joins after this time, the chat will be ended and an error message will be displayed to
the user. By default, there is no timeout.

ServiceDeskPublicConfig.allowReconnect.name: allowReconnect
ServiceDeskPublicConfig.allowReconnect.summary:
Indicates if Carbon AI Chat should automatically attempt to reconnect the user to a human agent when it is loaded. This
only works if the service desk integration being used supports reconnecting. This value defaults to true.

ServiceDeskPublicConfig.availabilityTime.name: availabilityTimeoutSeconds
ServiceDeskPublicConfig.availabilityTime.summary:
The timeout value in seconds to use when determining agent availability. When a connect_to_agent response is
received, the system will ask the service desk if any agents are available. If no response is received within
the timeout window, the system will return "false" to indicate no agents are available.

ServiceDeskPublicConfig.skipConnectHuman.name: skipConnectHumanAgentCard
ServiceDeskPublicConfig.skipConnectHuman.summary:
Indicates if Carbon AI Chat should auto-connect to an agent whenever it receives a connect_to_agent response and
agents are available. This essentially mimics the user clicking the "Request agent" button on the card. The
card is still displayed to the user.

SingleOption.name: SingleOption
SingleOption.summary: Represents an individual option that is part of an "options" response.

SingleOption.label.name: label
SingleOption.label.summary:
The user-facing label for the option or disambiguation suggestion. This label is taken from the user_label property
of the corresponding dialog node.

StartChatOptions.name: StartChatOptions
StartChatOptions.summary: Additional options that may be passed to the service desk when a chat is started.

StartChatOptions.preStartChatPayload.name: preStartChatPayload
StartChatOptions.preStartChatPayload.summary: Some arbitrary payload of data that was provided as part of the "human_agent:pre:startChat" event.

StructuredData.name: StructuredData
StructuredData.summary:
Structured data that can be sent alongside or instead of plain text input.
Supports typed fields for common input patterns (text, select, multi-select,
file, etc.) as well as an escape hatch for arbitrary user-defined data.

StructuredData.fields.name: fields
StructuredData.fields.summary: Typed fields with known structures (recommended for most use cases).

StructuredData.user_defined.name: user_defined
StructuredData.user_defined.summary: Escape hatch: arbitrary key-value data for user-defined implementations.

StructuredField.name: StructuredField
StructuredField.summary: A single typed field within a StructuredData payload.

StructuredFieldType.name: StructuredFieldType
StructuredFieldType.summary: The type of a structured field.

StructuredFieldValue.name: StructuredFieldValue
StructuredFieldValue.summary:
The value of a structured field. The actual type depends on the field's
StructuredFieldType.
StructuredFieldValue.signature: type StructuredFieldValue = string | number | boolean | string[] | FileFieldValue | FileFieldValue[] | any

SystemMessageItem.name: SystemMessageItem
SystemMessageItem.summary:
A system message item that can be returned in a message response. System messages are used for
status updates, progress indicators, or informational notices.

If a response contains ONLY system messages, they render standalone (centered, no avatar, no bubble).
If mixed with other response types, system messages render inline within the message bubble.

SystemMessageItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
SystemMessageItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

SystemMessageItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
SystemMessageItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

SystemMessageItem.response_type.name: response_type
SystemMessageItem.response_type.summary: The response type is always "system" for system messages.

SystemMessageItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
SystemMessageItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

SystemMessageItem.title.name: title
SystemMessageItem.title.summary: The title text to display in the system message.

SystemMessageItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
SystemMessageItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

SystemMessageItem.variant.name: variant
SystemMessageItem.variant.summary:
How the system line is presented when the message renders as a **standalone** system line
(response contains only system items): default helper text, date separator with rules, or
agent with a rule above and helper text. When system items render **inline** inside a
bubble, `variant` is ignored and the default inline style is used.

TextItem.name: TextItem
TextItem.summary:
A text item returned in a message response from an assistant.

The Carbon AI Chat supports basic styling inside text responses to match the theme of your Carbon AI Chat,
both with Markdown or HTML content returned from your assistant. Using Markdown and `user_defined`
(UserDefinedItem) responses instead of HTML in your text responses is the recommendation. It allows
adding channels that do not support HTML (such as Facebook, Slack, or WhatsApp) without having to rewrite
your content.

## Markdown

The Carbon AI Chat supports the following Markdown syntax in the text response type:

**Text formatting:**

- `**bold text**` or `__bold text__`
- `*italic text*` or `_italic text_`
- `~~strikethrough~~`
- `==highlighted text==`
- `^superscript^`
- `~subscript~`

**Code:**

- `` `inline code` `` or fenced code blocks with syntax highlighting.

**Headers:**

- `# H1`, `## H2`, `### H3`, `#### H4`, `##### H5`, `###### H6`

**Lists:**

- Unordered lists using `*`, `+`, or `-`
- Ordered lists using `1.`, `2.`, etc.
- Nested lists are supported

**Links and images:**

- `[link text](URL)` for links (opens in new tab by default)
- `![alt text](image URL)` for images

**Other elements:**

- `> blockquote text` for blockquotes
- Tables using pipe syntax with automatic pagination and sorting
- Horizontal rules using `---` or `***`
- Line breaks are preserved (breaks: true)
- Automatic URL detection and conversion to links

**Attributes:**

- Custom attributes using `{{class="my-class" id="my-id"}}` syntax
- Supported attributes: `target`, `rel`, `class`, `id`

**HTML support:**

- Raw HTML is supported when enabled
- Custom elements and web components are allowed
- Content is sanitized for security when sanitization is enabled

The Carbon AI Chat follows CommonMark rules with these extensions and enhancements.

## HTML content

If you include HTML (including `style` and `script` tags) in your text response from your assistant, the
Carbon AI Chat renders those elements as provided, unless you set PublicConfig.shouldSanitizeHTML
to `true`. A better approach is to use a `user_defined` response instead of adding HTML directly to your
responses to make adding support for channels that do not support HTML easier.

TextItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
TextItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

TextItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
TextItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

TextItem.response_type.name: response_type
TextItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

TextItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
TextItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

TextItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
TextItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

TokenTree.name: TokenTree
TokenTree.summary:
Markdown-it parser node tree, surfaced on the `node` field of
MarkdownRendererTableArgs and MarkdownRendererCodeBlockArgs
so custom renderers can inspect the parsed token structure when the
high-level data payload isn't enough.

TypeAndHandler.name: TypeAndHandler
TypeAndHandler.summary: The type of the object that is passed to the event bus functions (e.g. "on") when registering a handler.

TypeAndHandler.handler.name: handler
TypeAndHandler.handler.summary: The handler for events of this type.

TypeAndHandler.type.name: type
TypeAndHandler.type.summary: The type of event this handler is for.

UploadConfig.name: UploadConfig
UploadConfig.summary: Configuration for file upload behavior in the chat input.

UploadConfig.accept.name: accept
UploadConfig.accept.summary:
Accepted MIME types or file extensions, in the same format as the HTML `accept` attribute.
Examples: `"image/*"`, `".pdf,.docx"`, `"application/pdf"`.
If omitted, all file types are accepted.

UploadConfig.is_on.name: is_on
UploadConfig.is_on.summary:
Whether file upload is enabled. When `true`, the chat renders a file attachment button
in the input area. Defaults to `false`.

If `is_on` is `true` but `onFileUpload` is not provided, an error is logged and
file upload is disabled.

UploadConfig.maxFileSizeBytes.name: maxFileSizeBytes
UploadConfig.maxFileSizeBytes.summary:
Maximum file size in bytes. Files exceeding this limit are rejected client-side
before `onFileUpload` is called.

UploadConfig.maxFiles.name: maxFiles
UploadConfig.maxFiles.summary: Maximum number of files that can be attached at once. If omitted, there is no limit.

UploadConfig.onFileUpload.name: onFileUpload
UploadConfig.onFileUpload.summary:
Called once per file when the user selects it.

Return a StructuredData object representing this file's contribution to the
pending message. The widget merges the returned `StructuredData` into
`pendingStructuredData` and tracks it per-upload so that individual files can be
removed before the message is sent.

On failure: throw or return a rejected `Promise` — the widget marks the file as
errored in the UI.

UpsertMessageUpdater.name: UpsertMessageUpdater
UpsertMessageUpdater.summary:
The updater function passed to ChatInstanceMessaging.upsertMessage. Receives the
message currently stored under the target ID (or `undefined` when no message with that
ID is in the store) and returns the MessageResponse that should replace it. May
be synchronous or asynchronous.
UpsertMessageUpdater.signature: type UpsertMessageUpdater = (previous: MessageResponse | undefined) => Promise<MessageResponse> | MessageResponse

UserDefinedItem.name: UserDefinedItem
UserDefinedItem.summary: A user defined item returned in a message response from an assistant.

UserDefinedItem.agent_message_type.name: agent_message_type
UserDefinedItem.agent_message_type.summary: For messages that are sent between the user and a human agent, we assign an agent type to the message to distinguish what type it is.

UserDefinedItem.full_width.name: full_width
UserDefinedItem.full_width.summary: If the user_defined response type should be rendered as full width and ignore margin on the "start".

UserDefinedItem.message_item_options.name: message_item_options
UserDefinedItem.message_item_options.summary: Options that control additional features available for a message item.

UserDefinedItem.response_type.name: response_type
UserDefinedItem.response_type.summary: The response type of this message item.

UserDefinedItem.streaming_metadata.name: streaming_metadata
UserDefinedItem.streaming_metadata.summary:
Metadata used to identify a generic item within the context of a stream in order to correlate any updates meant
for a specific item.

UserDefinedItem.user_defined.name: user_defined
UserDefinedItem.user_defined.summary: An optional buckets of additional user defined properties for this item.

UserMessageErrorInfo.name: UserMessageErrorInfo
UserMessageErrorInfo.summary: This error is used to report when there was an error sending a message to the agent.

UserMessageErrorInfo.logInfo.name: logInfo
UserMessageErrorInfo.logInfo.summary: An optional value that will be logged to the console as an error.

UserMessageErrorInfo.messageID.name: messageID
UserMessageErrorInfo.messageID.summary: The ID of the message that is in error.

UserMessageErrorInfo.type.name: type
UserMessageErrorInfo.type.summary: The discriminating value for this type.

UserType.name: UserType
UserType.summary: Types of users we accept messages from.

UserType.BOT.name: BOT
UserType.BOT.summary:
A message from a non-watsonx assistant, used for interacting with assistants that are not backed by watsonx.

Official guidance is to not use this for IBM products without explicit exception.

VerticalCellAlignment.name: VerticalCellAlignment
VerticalCellAlignment.summary: Vertical alignment values for items in a grid response.

VideoItem.name: VideoItem
VideoItem.summary: The video response type definition for future reuse. This is currently the same as MediaItem.

ViewChangeReason.name: ViewChangeReason
ViewChangeReason.summary: The possible reasons why the view may be changed.

ViewChangeReason.CALLED_CHANGE_VIEW.name: CALLED_CHANGE_VIEW
ViewChangeReason.CALLED_CHANGE_VIEW.summary: Indicates the view was changed by a call to ChatInstance.changeView.

ViewChangeReason.LAUNCHER_CLICKED.name: LAUNCHER_CLICKED
ViewChangeReason.LAUNCHER_CLICKED.summary: Indicates the user clicked on our built-in launcher button that opened the main window.

ViewChangeReason.MAIN_WINDOW_MINIMIZED.name: MAIN_WINDOW_MINIMIZED
ViewChangeReason.MAIN_WINDOW_MINIMIZED.summary: Indicates the user clicked on our built-in minimize button that closed the launcher.

ViewChangeReason.WEB_CHAT_LOADED.name: WEB_CHAT_LOADED
ViewChangeReason.WEB_CHAT_LOADED.summary:
Indicates the Carbon AI Chat has loaded for the first time and a view is trying to open. If openChatByDefault is
true then the main window will be trying to open, otherwise the launcher will be trying to open.

ViewState.name: ViewState
ViewState.summary: Whether a particular Carbon AI Chat view is visible or not.

ViewState.launcher.name: launcher
ViewState.launcher.summary: Whether the launcher is visible or not.

ViewState.mainWindow.name: mainWindow
ViewState.mainWindow.summary: Whether the main window is visible or not.

ViewType.name: ViewType
ViewType.summary: The different views that can be shown by Carbon AI Chat.

ViewType.LAUNCHER.name: LAUNCHER
ViewType.LAUNCHER.summary: The launcher view is used to open the main window.

ViewType.MAIN_WINDOW.name: MAIN_WINDOW
ViewType.MAIN_WINDOW.summary:
The main window view is used to ask WA questions and converse with an agent, as well as many other things. The
string value is kept camel case to align with the viewState mainWindow property.

WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.name: WCCustomMarkdownRenderers
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.summary:
The web-component analogue of CustomMarkdownRenderers — same shape,
but each callback returns an `HTMLElement` (or `null`) instead of a React
node. Return `null` to opt out for a specific descriptor and use the
default Carbon rendering instead.

Callbacks fire once per matching element per render pass; return the same
element reference across renders to avoid unnecessary DOM churn.

WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock.name: codeBlock
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.codeBlock.summary:
Override the default rendering for fenced code blocks. Receives parsed
code-block data; return `null` to fall back to the default Carbon code
snippet renderer.

WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.table.name: table
WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.table.summary:
Override the default rendering for markdown tables. Receives parsed table
data; return `null` to fall back to the default Carbon table renderer.

WCMarkdown.name: WCMarkdown
WCMarkdown.summary:
Web-component-layer `markdown` config — extends PublicConfigMarkdown
with renderers returning `HTMLElement` (or `null`).

WCMarkdown.customRenderers.name: customRenderers
WCMarkdown.customRenderers.summary:
Per-element renderer overrides — see WCCustomMarkdownRenderers.
Return the same element reference across renders to avoid unnecessary DOM
churn.

WCMarkdown.markdownItPlugins.name: markdownItPlugins
WCMarkdown.markdownItPlugins.summary:
Markdown-it plugins applied after the built-in plugins
(markdown-it-attrs, markdown-it-highlight, markdown-it-task-lists).
Memoize this array — a new reference each render rebuilds the
markdown-it instance.

WCRenderCustomMessageFooter.name: WCRenderCustomMessageFooter
WCRenderCustomMessageFooter.summary:
The render function used to render a custom message footer in web
components. When provided, the library manages all event listening, slot
tracking, and element lifecycle. The callback receives the accumulated state
and should return an HTMLElement to display, or null to render nothing.

This is the web component analogue of RenderCustomMessageFooter and
mirrors the contract of WCRenderUserDefinedResponse.
WCRenderCustomMessageFooter.signature: type WCRenderCustomMessageFooter = (state: RenderCustomMessageFooterState, instance: ChatInstance) => HTMLElement | null

WCRenderUserDefinedResponse.name: WCRenderUserDefinedResponse
WCRenderUserDefinedResponse.summary:
The type of the render function used to render user defined responses in web components.
This function should return an HTMLElement to display for the given user defined state,
or null to render nothing.

The callback is invoked on every state update (new chunk, complete item, full message).
If you return the same element reference, the DOM is not disturbed. If you return a
new element, the previous content is replaced.
WCRenderUserDefinedResponse.signature: type WCRenderUserDefinedResponse = (state: RenderUserDefinedState, instance: ChatInstance) => HTMLElement | null

WidthOptions.LARGE.name: LARGE
WidthOptions.LARGE.summary: Max width of 585px, the full with of chat in fullscreen view with hasContentMaxWidth: true.

WidthOptions.MEDIUM.name: MEDIUM
WidthOptions.MEDIUM.summary: Max width of 438px, 2/3rd of the width of chat in fullscreen view with hasContentMaxWidth: true.

WidthOptions.SMALL.name: SMALL
WidthOptions.SMALL.summary: Width the size of the floating chat for smaller content.

WithBodyAndFooter.body.name: body
WithBodyAndFooter.body.summary: A list of message items to render in a Carbon AI Chat panel.

WithBodyAndFooter.footer.name: footer
WithBodyAndFooter.footer.summary: A list of button items that are rendered under the panel body.

WithWidthOptions.max_width.name: max_width
WithWidthOptions.max_width.summary:
Sets an optional max width of the component. Options are small, medium and large.
By default, the component will be 100% width of the container.

WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.name: WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions
WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.summary: Options supported by the workspace custom panel implementation.

WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.additi.name: additionalData
WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.additi.summary: Additional metadata associated with the workspace. This will be included in WORKSPACE_PRE_CLOSE and WORKSPACE_CLOSE events.

WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.prefer.name: preferredLocation
WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.prefer.summary: Where the chat will attempt to render the workspace in logical terms. For a ltr layout "start" will render on the left and "end" will render on the right. If there is not enough room to render the workspace, it will be rendered as a panel overlaying the content with a back button.

WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.title.name: title
WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.title.summary: The title of the workspace. Used for accessibility announcements.

WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.worksp.name: workspaceId
WorkspaceCustomPanelConfigOptions.worksp.summary: The ID of the workspace being opened. This will be included in WORKSPACE_PRE_CLOSE and WORKSPACE_CLOSE events.

WriteableElementName.AFTER_INPUT_ELEMENT.name: AFTER_INPUT_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.AFTER_INPUT_ELEMENT.summary: An element that appears after the input field.

WriteableElementName.AI_TOOLTIP_AFTER_DE.name: AI_TOOLTIP_AFTER_DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.AI_TOOLTIP_AFTER_DE.summary:
An element that appears in the AI theme only and is shown beneath the title and description in the AI tooltip
content.
WriteableElementName.AI_TOOLTIP_AFTER_DE.deprecatedMessage:
Use WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_CONTENT
and WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_ACTIONS for full control over AI label popover content.

WriteableElementName.BEFORE_INPUT_ELEMEN.name: BEFORE_INPUT_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.BEFORE_INPUT_ELEMEN.summary: An element that appears after the messages area and before the input area.

WriteableElementName.CUSTOM_PANEL_ELEMEN.name: CUSTOM_PANEL_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.CUSTOM_PANEL_ELEMEN.summary: An element to be housed in the custom panel.

WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPO.name: EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_ACTIONS
WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPO.summary: An element that appears in the header's AI label popover actions footer area.

WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPO.name: EXPLAINABILITY_POPOVER_CONTENT
WriteableElementName.EXPLAINABILITY_POPO.summary:
An element that appears in the header's AI label popover body. When content is provided to this slot,
`HeaderConfig.hideDefaultAiLabelContent` should be set to true.

WriteableElementName.FOOTER_ELEMENT.name: FOOTER_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.FOOTER_ELEMENT.summary: An element that appears in the footer area.

WriteableElementName.HEADER_BOTTOM_ELEME.name: HEADER_BOTTOM_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HEADER_BOTTOM_ELEME.summary: An element that appears in the header on a new line. Only visible while talking to the assistant.

WriteableElementName.HEADER_FIXED_ACTION.name: HEADER_FIXED_ACTIONS_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HEADER_FIXED_ACTION.summary: An element that appears in the header's fixed-actions slot (before close/minimize buttons).

WriteableElementName.HISTORY_PANEL_ELEME.name: HISTORY_PANEL_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HISTORY_PANEL_ELEME.summary: An element to be housed in the history panel.

WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_AFTER_S.name: HOME_SCREEN_AFTER_STARTERS_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_AFTER_S.summary: An element that appears on the home screen after the conversation starters.

WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_BEFORE_.name: HOME_SCREEN_BEFORE_INPUT_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_BEFORE_.summary: An element that appears above the input field on the home screen.

WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_HEADER_.name: HOME_SCREEN_HEADER_BOTTOM_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.HOME_SCREEN_HEADER_.summary: An element that appears on the home screen above the welcome message and conversation starters.

WriteableElementName.WELCOME_NODE_BEFORE.name: WELCOME_NODE_BEFORE_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.WELCOME_NODE_BEFORE.summary: An element that appears in the main message body directly above the welcome node.

WriteableElementName.WORKSPACE_PANEL_ELE.name: WORKSPACE_PANEL_ELEMENT
WriteableElementName.WORKSPACE_PANEL_ELE.summary: An element to be housed in the workspace panel.

WriteableElements.name: WriteableElements
WriteableElements.summary:
An object of elements we expose to developers to write to. Be sure to check the documentation of the React or
web component you are using for how to make use of this, as it differs based on implementation.

loadAllLazyDeps.name: loadAllLazyDeps
loadAllLazyDeps.summary:
Eagerly loads every lazily imported dependency across both
`@carbon/ai-chat-components` and `@carbon/ai-chat` so tests can preload
everything they need (Jest, Vitest, server rendering, etc.). Only available
from `@carbon/ai-chat/server`.

readCarbonChatSession.name: readCarbonChatSession
readCarbonChatSession.summary:
Reads and validates the Carbon AI Chat session from sessionStorage.
Returns null if no session exists, if the data is corrupt, or if the
session was written by a different version of the library (version mismatch).

Pass the same namespace value as PublicConfig.namespace (if any).
